In the first round of this auction, each buyer picks a price premium from a menu offered by the seller (a smaller premium costs more). Then the seller releases the additional signals. In the second round, the buyers bid in a second-price auction where the winner pays the sum of his premium and the second highest non-negative bid. In the case of a single buyer, this mechanism simplifies to a menu of European call options.</p>
papers.summary_en: A seller has more information than her buyers about a good. Should she release that information before an auction? This paper shows the answer is unambiguous: release everything. In the revenue-maximising mechanism, the seller discloses all signals she can, and her expected revenue equals what she would earn if she could privately observe the new information herself. The authors propose a "handicap auction" that implements this mechanism: buyers first pick a price premium from a menu, then the seller reveals information, then a second-price auction runs with premium adjustments. For single-buyer cases, it reduces to a menu of call options.
papers.summary_hu: Egy eladó több információval rendelkezik egy termékről, mint a vásárlók. Ki kellene-e adnia azt az információt egy aukció előtt? A tanulmány egyértelmű választ ad: adj ki mindent. A bevétel-maximalizáló mechanizmusban az eladó nyilvánosságra hozza az összes jelet, amit tud, és várható bevétele egyenlő azzal, amit akkor kapna, ha az új információt saját maga figyelhetné meg. A szerzők egy „handicap-aukciót" javasolnak, amely megvalósítja ezt a mechanizmust: a vásárlók először ár-prémiumot választanak egy menüből, majd az eladó közzéteszi az információt, majd egy második-ár-aukció fut a prémium-kiigazításokkal. Egyetlen vásárló esetén ez egy call-opciós menüre redukálódik.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; nincs empirikus adat.
papers.policy_relevance: Important for Hungarian public-asset disposal (Ministry of National Assets), spectrum auctions (NMHH), and EU-procurement rules: the paper's message is that more disclosure is revenue-improving, not revenue-destroying, when the auction mechanism is chosen well. Suggests a simple implementation via two-stage handicap auctions that could be piloted for public-land or property sales. Abstract setting; specific implementation needs mechanism design tuned to the asset.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Fontos a magyar közvagyon-eladáshoz (Nemzeti Vagyonügyi Minisztérium), spektrumaukciókhoz (NMHH) és EU-közbeszerzési szabályokhoz: a tanulmány üzenete, hogy a több közzététel bevétel-javító, nem -romboló, ha az aukciós mechanizmust jól választjuk. Egy egyszerű megvalósításra utal a kétlépcsős handicap-aukció révén, amit közföldek vagy ingatlanok eladásához lehetne tesztelni. Absztrakt környezet; konkrét megvalósításhoz mechanizmustervezés kell az eszközre szabva.

papers.title: Dynamic contracting: An irrelevance theorem
papers.abstract: This paper generalizes a conceptual insight in dynamic contracting with quasilinear payoffs: the principal does not need to pay any information rents for extracting the agent's “new” private information obtained after signing the contract. This is shown in a general model in which the agent's type stochastically evolves over time, and her payoff (which is linear in transfers) depends on the entire history of private and any contractible information, contractible decisions, and her hidden actions. The contract is offered by the principal in the presence of initial informational asymmetry. The model can be transformed into an equivalent one where the agent's subsequent information is independent in each period (type orthogonalization). We show that for any fixed decision–action rule implemented by a mechanism, the agent's rents (as well as the principal's maximal revenue) are the same as if the principal could observe and contract on the agent's orthogonalized types after the initial period. We also show that any monotonic decision–action rule can be implemented in a Markovian environment satisfying certain regularity conditions, and we provide a simple “recipe” for solving such dynamic contracting problems.
papers.summary_en: This paper proves a general "irrelevance" result for dynamic contracts: when agents' private information evolves over time, a principal (say, an employer or regulator) does not have to pay the agent to reveal new information acquired after signing. A clever reparameterisation ("type orthogonalisation") shows that the principal can extract all subsequent private information for free while still paying rents on the initial private information. The result unifies a large class of dynamic mechanism-design problems and provides a "recipe" for solving them.
papers.summary_hu: A tanulmány általános „irrelevancia" eredményt bizonyít a dinamikus szerződéselméletre: amikor az ügynökök magán-információja idővel fejlődik, a megbízónak (pl. munkáltató vagy szabályozó) nem kell fizetnie az ügynöknek azért, hogy felfedje a szerződéskötés után szerzett új információkat. Egy ügyes reparametrizálás („típus-ortogonalizálás") megmutatja, hogy a megbízó minden későbbi magán-információt ingyen kivonhat, miközben továbbra is fizet járulékot a kezdeti magán-információért. Az eredmény egyesíti a dinamikus mechanizmustervezési problémák széles osztályát, és „receptet" ad a megoldásukhoz.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; nincs empirikus adat.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to long-term regulatory contracts, public-private partnerships, and procurement with uncertain future states — all standard Hungarian settings. Examples: concessions for motorway operations, long-term healthcare-provision contracts, energy-regulation tariffs. The takeaway: sophisticated, state-contingent contracting can eliminate the "information rents" that bureaucrats and regulators commonly assume they must pay. A theoretical-methodological contribution; direct translation to policy requires design expertise.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Releváns hosszú távú szabályozási szerződésekhez, köz-magán partnerségekhez és bizonytalan jövőbeli állapotokkal való közbeszerzéshez — mind standard magyar helyzetek. Példák: autópálya-üzemeltetési koncessziók, hosszú távú egészségügyi szolgáltatói szerződések, energiaszabályozási tarifák. Üzenet: a szofisztikált, állapot-kontingens szerződéskötés képes kiküszöbölni azokat az „információs járulékokat", amelyeket a hivatalnokok és szabályozók gyakran fizetendőnek vélnek. Elméleti-módszertani hozzájárulás; közvetlen szakpolitikai átvitel tervezési szakértelmet igényel.

papers.title: Bargaining With Hard Evidence
papers.abstract: Abstract This article studies how the presence of concealable hard evidence affects the timing of agreement and the size and distribution of surplus in bargaining. A buyer and a seller receive randomly arriving, verifiable, but concealable evidence about the value of a tradable good. Each party discloses individually favourable information but conceals signals that benefit the other side, giving rise to mutual suspicion; the seller repeatedly posts prices valid for one period. In the leading case of interest with a finite horizon and sufficiently patient players, the equilibrium is characterised by an interval of skimming (a sequence of prices acceptable only to a buyer who has learned that the good’s value is high) concluded by a single settlement period in which agreement is reached for sure. The length of delay until agreement and the corresponding efficiency loss are decreasing in the time horizon and in the abilities of the trading parties to identify the good’s value, but increasing in impatience. An arbitrarily long time horizon leads to immediate agreement if the parties are even slightly impatient and interaction is frequent enough; if the parties are perfectly patient, there is no trade without evidence disclosure.
papers.summary_en: In many negotiations, both sides can acquire private, verifiable information they can choose to reveal or conceal. Each party reveals info favourable to themselves and hides info favourable to the other — generating mutual suspicion. This paper analyses the bargaining outcome. In the main case (finite horizon, patient players), equilibrium has a "skimming" phase — a sequence of progressively lower prices accepted only by buyers who have learned the good is high-value — followed by a single settlement period in which agreement is reached for sure. Delay and its efficiency loss fall as info-acquisition costs fall. A clean model of how hard evidence shapes real bargaining.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian contract-dispute, procurement, and merger-negotiation contexts where both parties can credibly signal private information. The paper supports procedural rules that reduce the cost of information disclosure (for example, structured due diligence in public procurement). Relevant to Közbeszerzési Hatóság (Public Procurement Office). Caveat: theoretical; identifying the "hard evidence" dimension empirically in Hungarian procurement is non-trivial.

papers.title: Precautionary Bidding in Auctions
papers.abstract: We analyze bidding behavior in auctions when risk-averse bidders bid for an object whose value is risky. We show that, as risk increases, decreasingly risk-averse bidders will reduce their bids by more than the risk premium. Ceteris paribus, bidders will be better off bidding for a more risky object in first-price, second-price, English, and allpay auctions with affiliated private values. We then extend the results to common value settings. This 'precautionary bidding' effect arises because the expected marginal utility of income increases with risk, so bidders are reluctant to bid so highly. We show that precautionary bidding also arises in response to common values risk. This precautionary bidding behavior can make decreasingly risk-averse bidders better off when they face a 'winner's curse' than when they do not.
papers.summary_en: What happens in auctions when the object being sold has uncertain value and bidders are risk-averse? Conventional wisdom says bidders will bid lower to compensate for the risk premium. This paper shows they go further: "precautionary bidding" means bidders reduce their bids by more than the risk premium, because the expected marginal utility of income rises when income is riskier. Counter-intuitively, bidders are better off bidding for a riskier object — in first-price, second-price, English, and all-pay auctions. The effect also arises in common-value settings and can make risk-averse bidders better off in winner's-curse environments. A foundational contribution to auction theory.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian government-auction design (privatisations, spectrum auctions, treasury-bond auctions). The result implies that when valuing something is uncertain (resource rights, new spectrum licences), bidders with risk aversion bid more cautiously than a naive risk-premium adjustment suggests; auction revenue estimates should account for this. Relevant to MNB debt-auction design and to GVH/EU-compatible infrastructure auctions. Caveat: theoretical; the quantitative size of precautionary bidding depends on bidders' risk attitudes, which must be estimated separately.

papers.title: Estimation of Conditional Average Treatment Effects With High-Dimensional Data
papers.journal: Journal of Business and Economic Statistics
papers.abstract: Given the unconfoundedness assumption, we propose new nonparametric estimators for the reduced dimensional conditional average treatment effect (CATE) function. In the first stage, the nuisance functions necessary for identifying CATE are estimated by machine learning methods, allowing the number of covariates to be comparable to or larger than the sample size. The second stage consists of a low-dimensional local linear regression, reducing CATE to a function of the covariate(s) of interest. We consider two variants of the estimator depending on whether the nuisance functions are estimated over the full sample or over a hold-out sample. Building on Belloni at al. and Chernozhukov et al., we derive functional limit theory for the estimators and provide an easy-to-implement procedure for uniform inference based on the multiplier bootstrap. The empirical application revisits the effect of maternal smoking on a baby’s birth weight as a function of the mother’s age.
papers.summary_en: When estimating how a program's effect varies with observable characteristics, how should we handle the fact that many covariates might matter but we don't know which? This methodological paper develops a two-stage nonparametric estimator. The first stage uses machine learning to estimate the needed nuisance functions (allowing for high-dimensional covariates); the second stage uses low-dimensional local regression on the covariate of interest. The approach is efficient, allows proper inference, and is demonstrated on maternal-smoking effects on birth weight as a function of mother's age.
papers.summary_hu: Amikor azt becsüljük, hogyan változik egy program hatása megfigyelhető tulajdonságok szerint, hogyan kezeljük azt a tényt, hogy sok kovariáns számíthat, de nem tudjuk, melyik? A módszertani tanulmány kétlépcsős nem-parametrikus becslőt fejleszt. Az első lépésben gépi tanulás becsli a szükséges zavaró függvényeket (magas-dimenziós kovariánsokat engedve); a második lépés alacsony-dimenziós lokális regressziót használ az érdeklődésre számot tartó kovariánsra. A megközelítés hatékony, megfelelő következtetést enged, és az anyai dohányzás-születési-súly hatásán illusztrálják az anya életkorának függvényében.
papers.data_used: Methodological paper; empirical application uses US natality data on birth weight and maternal characteristics.
papers.data_used_hu: Módszertani tanulmány; az empirikus alkalmazás amerikai születési adatokat használ a születési súlyról és az anya tulajdonságairól.
papers.policy_relevance: Useful for Hungarian policy evaluation when heterogeneous effects matter (which is almost always). For researchers at KRTK, MNB, and ministries evaluating program impacts: this estimator lets you use modern machine-learning tools while still getting proper standard errors on effect heterogeneity. Particularly relevant for evaluating labor-market programs, health interventions, and education reforms where effects likely differ across subpopulations.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Hasznos a magyar szakpolitikai értékeléshez, amikor heterogén hatások számítanak (ami majdnem mindig igaz). A KRTK, MNB és minisztériumok programértékelő kutatóinak: ez a becslő modern gépi-tanulási eszközök használatát engedi, miközben megfelelő standard hibákat ad a hatás-heterogenitásra. Különösen releváns munkaerőpiaci programok, egészségügyi beavatkozások és oktatási reformok értékelésénél, ahol a hatások valószínűleg alpopulációnként különböznek.

papers.title: Heterogeneous Global Booms and Busts
papers.abstract: We investigate the heterogeneous boom and bust patterns across countries that emerge as a result of global shocks. Our analysis sheds light on the emergence of core and periphery countries, and the joint determination of the depth of recessions and tightness of credit markets across countries. The model implies that interest rates are similar across core and periphery countries in booms, with larger credit and output growth in periphery countries. However, a common global shock that leads to a credit crunch across the globe gives rise to a sharper spike in interest rates and a deeper recession in periphery countries, while a credit flight to the core alleviates the adverse consequences in these countries. We explore the implication of the model about credit spreads, portfolio rebalancing, investment, non-performing debt and concentration of debt ownership during booms and busts, both in the time series and in the cross-section, and connect them to existing stylized facts. We further demonstrate how the anatomy of the global economy evolves as a result of aggregate demand and supply shocks to financing, such as a global saving glut.Institutional subscribers to the NBER working paper series, and residents of developing countries may download this paper without additional charge at www.nber.org.
papers.summary_en: Why do some countries experience deeper recessions and sharper credit crunches during global busts? This paper builds a model where global financial shocks hit differently in "core" and "periphery" economies. During booms, interest rates look similar across countries but credit and output grow faster in the periphery. During busts, a common global shock produces a sharper rate spike and deeper recession in the periphery, while a "flight to core" alleviates the core's pain. The paper matches a wide range of cross-country facts about credit spreads, portfolio rebalancing, investment, and debt concentration over the global business cycle.
papers.data_used: Theoretical model calibrated to cross-country macro-financial data: credit spreads, output, investment, non-performing loans, debt ownership concentration, over global booms and busts.
papers.policy_relevance: Hungary is a textbook "periphery" economy in this model — exposed to global credit cycles with potentially sharp recessions when flight-to-quality episodes occur (2008–09, COVID-19). Directly relevant to MNB's macroprudential policy, foreign-currency debt exposure management, and the case for capital buffers. The paper supports a systematic precautionary stance: even if a Hungarian recession is caused by a global shock, the amplitude depends on buffers built in good times. Caveat: the calibration is cross-country; Hungary-specific parameters (banking-system openness, forint regime) would change the exact quantitative prescriptions.

papers.title: Cleansing by tight credit: Rational cycles and endogenous lending standards
papers.abstract: Endogenous cycles emerge through the two-way interaction between lending standards and production fundamentals. Lax lending standards in booms lead to low interest rates and high output but the deterioration of future loan quality. Low borrower quality in turn precipitates tight standards: the economy enters a recession with high credit spreads and low output but a gradual improvement in the quality of loans. This eventually triggers a shift back to a boom with lax lending, and the cycle continues. The capitalization of expert investors determines the strength of capital reallocation in recessions. Furthermore, although the constrained efficient economy is often cyclical, it features both a static and a dynamic externality in credit supply, hence differing from the decentralized equilibrium.
papers.summary_en: Credit cycles — periods of lax then tight lending — aren't just driven by shocks from outside. This paper shows how lax standards in a boom gradually deteriorate loan quality, precipitating tight standards in the next bust. During the bust, loan quality improves (the "cleansing" effect), which eventually triggers a return to lax standards. The cycle self-generates. The paper also shows that expert investors' capitalisation determines how quickly credit gets reallocated in recessions, and identifies both static and dynamic externalities that make the decentralised equilibrium differ from the constrained optimum. A sophisticated framework for macro-financial policy.
papers.data_used: Theoretical macro-finance model with calibrated extensions; predictions match cross-country credit-cycle stylized facts.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly informs MNB's macroprudential policy. The paper supports: countercyclical capital buffers that lean against lax lending in booms (to reduce the depth of the next bust); regulation of expert-investor market power to avoid slow recession reallocation; and recognition that credit-supply externalities mean the decentralised equilibrium is socially inefficient — so some form of active macroprudential intervention is justified even without explicit market failures. Relevant to MNB's Counter-Cyclical Capital Buffer setting and systemic-risk monitoring. Caveat: calibration is US/global; Hungarian magnitudes depend on banking-sector concentration and foreign-bank ownership.

papers.title: Revealed Comparative Advantage and Competitiveness in Hungarian Agri–Food Sectors
papers.abstract: We examine the competitiveness of Hungarian agriculture and food processing, in relation to that of the EU, based on four indices of revealed comparative advantage, using highly disaggregate data for the period 1992 to 1998. Consistency tests suggest that the indices are less satisfactory as cardinal measures, but are useful in identifying the demarcation between comparative advantage and comparative disadvantage. Hungary is shown to have a comparative advantage in a range of agri‐food products, including animals and meat. This complements the findings of those studies that have used price and cost based approaches in identifying competitiveness in cereals and crops. Results indicate that the RCA indices, when interpreted as a binary measure, have remained surprisingly stable during the period of transition, although there is evidence of a weakening in the level of comparative advantage as revealed in the Balassa index.
papers.summary_en: How competitive is Hungarian agriculture compared to the rest of the EU? This paper uses four different indices of revealed comparative advantage on 1992–1998 data, asking whether Hungary has a persistent advantage in particular products. Findings: Hungary has comparative advantage in a range of agri-food products, notably animals and meat. The RCA indices work better as binary indicators than as precise cardinal measures, but the underlying competitive position is stable over the 1990s. A foundational analysis for understanding Hungary's agricultural-export portfolio.
papers.summary_hu: Mennyire versenyképes a magyar mezőgazdaság az EU többi részéhez képest? A tanulmány négy különböző feltárt komparatív előny indexet használ 1992–1998 adatokon, és azt kérdezi, hogy Magyarország rendelkezik-e tartós előnnyel bizonyos termékekben. Eredmények: Magyarországnak komparatív előnye van több agrár-élelmiszertermékben, különösen állatokban és húsban. Az RCA indexek jobban működnek bináris mutatóként, mint pontos kardinális mértékként, de a mögöttes versenypozíció stabil az 1990-es évek során. Alapvető elemzés Magyarország agrár-exportportfóliójának megértéséhez.
papers.data_used: Highly disaggregated Hungarian agri-food trade data, 1992–1998. Four revealed comparative advantage indices compared.
papers.data_used_hu: Erősen dezaggregált magyar agrár-élelmiszer kereskedelmi adatok, 1992–1998. Négy feltárt komparatív előny index összehasonlítva.
papers.policy_relevance: Foundational for Hungarian CAP-implementation and agricultural-export strategy. The paper identifies livestock and meat as Hungary's comparative-advantage areas — a position that has since shifted. Relevant to historical context for agricultural-policy reform. Caveat: 1990s data; the Hungarian agricultural sector has undergone substantial structural change since, and current comparative advantages may be quite different.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Alapvető a magyar KAP-végrehajtáshoz és agrár-export-stratégiához. A tanulmány azonosítja a haszonállat-tenyésztést és a húsipart, mint Magyarország komparatív előny területeit — olyan pozíciót, amely azóta változott. Releváns mint történelmi kontextus az agrárpolitikai reformhoz. Figyelmeztetés: 1990-es évek adatai; a magyar mezőgazdasági szektor azóta jelentős szerkezetváltozáson ment keresztül, és a jelenlegi komparatív előnyök teljesen mások lehetnek.

papers.title: Drivers of vertical intra-industry trade: the case of the Hungarian agri-food sector
papers.abstract: This article investigates the drivers of vertical intra-industry trade (VIIT) in Hungarian agri-food trade with the European Union (EU). It identifies three possible ways to measure intra-industry trade (IIT) flows (GHM, FF, and N methods) and defines six hypotheses to test for the drivers of VIIT with three panel data models (static, dynamic, and FEVD). The results suggest that factor endowments are negatively, while economic size is positively and significantly related to VIIT. Distance and VIIT were found to be negatively related as is commonly the case in the standard gravity model. It was also found that VIIT is greater if a New Member State (NMS) is exporting agri-food produce to an NMS, while EU accession has ambiguously influenced the share of VIIT. In general, it seems that our results are independent from model estimations and interestingly they do not differ considerably as we a priori expected. Moreover, our results seem surprisingly robust across various measurements of ITT.
papers.summary_en: Vertical intra-industry trade (exporting and importing goods of different quality within the same product category) is economically informative: it reflects product differentiation and specialisation. This paper asks what drives vertical IIT in Hungarian agri-food trade with the EU. Three drivers matter: factor endowments (negative), economic size (positive), and distance (negative — consistent with gravity-model intuitions). New-member-state exports to other new-member-states show higher vertical IIT shares, and EU accession had an ambiguous effect. A careful empirical portrait of trade-in-variety in Hungarian agri-food.
papers.data_used: Hungarian agri-food trade data with EU partners. Multiple IIT measurement methods (GHM, FF, N) tested. Panel-data models (static, dynamic, FEVD).
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian agri-food export policy and to EU new-member-state trade-policy debates. The paper's message: trade in differentiated agri-food products is sensitive to distance and partner-country size — supporting Hungary's focus on regional markets (V4, Balkans) for specialised exports. Relevant to HEPA and NAK export strategy. Caveat: pre-2014 data; product differentiation and quality upgrading have continued.

papers.title: The power ranking of the members of the Agricultural Committee of the European Parliament
papers.abstract: Abstract We aim to identify the most influential members of the Agricultural Committee of the European Parliament (COMAGRI). Unlike previous studies that were based on case studies or interviews with stakeholders, we analyse the voting power of MEPs using a spatial Banzhaf power index. We identify critical members: members whose votes are necessary to form winning coalitions. We found that rapporteurs, EP group coordinators and MEPs from countries with high relative Committee representations, such as Ireland, Poland or Romania are powerful actors. Italy emerges as the most influential member state, while France seems surprisingly weak.
papers.summary_en: Who are the most influential MEPs on EU agricultural policy? This paper uses formal voting-power analysis (spatial Banzhaf index) to rank members of the European Parliament's Agricultural Committee. It identifies "critical members" whose votes are necessary to form winning coalitions. Findings: rapporteurs, EP group coordinators, and MEPs from countries with disproportionate committee representation (Ireland, Poland, Romania) are the most powerful. Italy emerges as the most influential member state; France is surprisingly weak. A quantitative complement to previous case-study work on EU agricultural lobbying.
papers.data_used: Voting records and coalition structures of the European Parliament's Agricultural Committee (COMAGRI), multiple years. Spatial Banzhaf power index calculation.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian EU-affairs strategy (MFM, NAK). The paper shows which MEP positions (rapporteur, EP group coordinator) are genuinely powerful — a guide for Hungarian political investment in securing key committee appointments. Relevant to EU agricultural-policy advocacy. Caveat: voting-power indices have well-known limitations; actual influence depends on issue salience and political context not captured in the index.

papers.title: Early Marriage and Female Schooling in Bangladesh
papers.abstract: This paper provides empirical evidence of the influence of adolescent marriage opportunities on female schooling attainment and gives predictions of the impact of imposing universal age-of-consent laws. Using data from rural Bangladesh, we explore the commonly cited hypotheses that women attain less schooling as a result of marrying young. We isolate the causal effect of marriage timing by exploiting variation in the timing of menarche as an instrumental variable for age of first marriage. Our results indicate that marriage age matters: Each additional year that marriage is delayed is associated with 0.30 additional years of schooling and 6.5% higher probability of literacy. Delayed marriage is also associated with a significant increase in use of preventive health care services, some of which appears to be independent of the change in schooling, indicating separate “age effects” of delaying marriage. In the context of competitive marriage markets we show that the above results can be used to obtain estimates of the change in equilibrium female education that would arise from introducing a minimum legal age of marriage. The resulting analysis implies that, under reasonable assumptions, enforcing universal age of consent laws would have a strong positive impact on female schooling. JEL Classification: I32, I12, J12, J13, J16, O12
papers.summary_en: Girls in rural Bangladesh who marry young get less education. Is this just because school drop-outs marry sooner, or does early marriage actually cause lower schooling? This paper uses variation in the timing of menarche (puberty) as an instrumental variable for age of first marriage, isolating the causal effect. Result: each additional year of marriage delay raises schooling by 0.30 years and literacy probability by 6.5 percentage points. Delayed marriage also improves use of preventive health care. A pivotal result for global campaigns to raise the legal age of marriage.
papers.data_used: Survey data from rural Bangladesh; instrumental-variable strategy using exogenous variation in age of menarche as instrument for age of first marriage.
papers.policy_relevance: Limited direct policy relevance in Hungary (legal marriage age is already 18 and early marriage is uncommon). The paper is relevant to Hungary's international development assistance and to EU external cooperation on gender equality. Methodologically useful for any research on age-related policy effects (school-entry age, compulsory-schooling age) using biomedical instruments. Caveat: the Bangladesh context is specific; the quantitative magnitudes won't apply elsewhere.

papers.title: Early Marriage, Age of Menarche, and Female Schooling Attainment in Bangladesh
papers.abstract: (Article begins on next page) The Harvard community has made this article openly available. Please share how this access benefits you. Your story matters.
papers.summary_en: In much of the world girls are still married very young. This paper uses an unusual natural experiment from Bangladesh — variation in the age of menarche — to estimate the causal effect of delaying marriage on girls' educational attainment. Girls who reached menarche one year later married about 6 months later, and each additional year of postponed marriage raised schooling by 0.22 years and literacy rates by 5.6 percentage points. The result implies that ending child marriage could substantially raise female education — a strong, country-specific causal estimate on an important global policy question.
papers.summary_hu: A világ nagy részén a lányok még mindig nagyon fiatalon házasodnak. A tanulmány egy szokatlan természetes kísérletet használ Bangladesből — a menarché-kor variációja — hogy megbecsülje a házasságkötés késleltetésének oksági hatását a lányok iskolázottságára. Azok a lányok, akik egy évvel később értek menarchéba, körülbelül fél évvel később házasodtak, és minden egyes elhalasztott évnyi házasság 0,22 évvel emelte az iskolázottságot és 5,6 százalékponttal az írástudási arányt. Az eredmény azt sugallja, hogy a gyermekházasság megszüntetése érdemben emelhetné a női oktatást — erős, ország-specifikus oksági becslés egy fontos globális szakpolitikai kérdésről.
papers.data_used: Household survey of 1,400 married women in 125 villages in rural Bangladesh collected by the authors; retrospective data on age of menarche, age at marriage, and schooling.
papers.data_used_hu: Háztartási felmérés 1 400 házas nőről 125 vidéki bangladesi faluban, amit a szerzők gyűjtöttek; visszamenőleges adatok a menarché-korról, házasságkötési korról és iskolázottságról.
papers.policy_relevance: Primary relevance is to policy in lower-income countries with prevalent child marriage, not directly to Hungary. For Hungarian development-assistance policy (Hungary Helps, IVF cooperation programmes), provides rigorous evidence that legal and normative interventions to delay marriage can pay off in girls' education. Setting-specific: the age-of-menarche instrument is not a policy lever itself; the causal estimate informs what the returns to delaying marriage would be if other levers (education, legal age) succeed.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Az elsődleges relevancia az alacsonyabb jövedelmű országokban érvényesülő gyermekházasság-politikához kötődik, nem közvetlenül Magyarországhoz. A magyar fejlesztési támogatási politikához (Hungary Helps, V4-együttműködési programok) szigorú bizonyítékot ad arra, hogy a házasságkötést késleltető jogi és normatív beavatkozások megtérülhetnek a lányok oktatásában. Szituáció-specifikus: a menarché-kor instrumentum maga nem szakpolitikai eszköz; az oksági becslés arról tájékoztat, mik lennének a hozamok, ha más eszközök (oktatás, jogi életkor) sikerrel járnának.

papers.title: A Fixed-Point Approach to Stable Matchings and Some Applications
papers.abstract: We describe a fixed-point based approach to the theory of bipartite stable matchings. By this, we provide a common framework that links together seemingly distant results, like the stable marriage theorem of Gale and Shapley, the Mendelsohn-Dulmage theorem, the Kundu-Lawler theorem, Tarski's fixed-point theorem, the Cantor-Bernstein theorem, Pym's linking theorem, or the monochromatic path theorem of Sands et al. In this framework, we formulate a matroid-generalization of the stable marriage theorem and study the lattice structure of generalized stable matchings. Based on the theory of lattice polyhedra and blocking polyhedra, we extend results of Vande Vate and Rothblum on the bipartite stable matching polytope.

papers.title: A constrained independent set problem for matroids
Trading Networks With Frictions
papers.abstract: We show how frictions and continuous transfers jointly affect equilibria in a model of matching in trading networks. Our model incorporates distortionary frictions such as transaction taxes and commissions. When contracts are fully substitutable for firms, competitive equilibria exist and coincide with outcomes that satisfy a cooperative solution concept called trail stability . However, competitive equilibria are generally neither stable nor Pareto‐efficient.
papers.summary_en: Trading networks (think: supply chains or financial markets) involve many firms exchanging contracts with continuous monetary transfers. What happens when transactions are also taxed or commissioned? This paper builds a matching-with-frictions model that handles this case. When contracts are "substitutable" for firms, competitive equilibria exist and coincide with "trail-stable" outcomes. A sobering finding, though: competitive equilibria with frictions are typically neither stable in the usual matching-theory sense nor Pareto-efficient. This characterises when pricing distortions damage network outcomes and how deep that damage goes.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to any Hungarian market with many-to-many contracting under taxation (supply chains facing VAT or excise taxes; financial intermediation under transaction taxes). The paper formalises why transaction taxes can cause network rearrangements that depart from efficient matching — a concern for the EU digital-services tax and financial transaction tax debates. Caveat: the model's substitutability assumption is strong and must be verified in real markets before concluding a specific Hungarian tax has these distorting effects.

papers.title: Complexity of stability in trading networks
papers.abstract: Abstract Efficient computability is an important property of solution concepts. We consider the computational complexity of finding and verifying various solution concepts in trading networks—multi-sided matching markets with bilateral contracts and without transferable utility—under the assumption of full substitutability of agents’ preferences. It is known that outcomes that satisfy trail stability always exist and can be found in linear time. However, we show that the existence of stable outcomes—immune to deviations by arbitrary sets of agents—is an $${{\textsf{N}}}{{\textsf{P}}}$$ <mml:math xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"> <mml:mrow> <mml:mi>N</mml:mi> <mml:mi>P</mml:mi> </mml:mrow> </mml:math> -hard problem in trading networks. We also show that even verifying whether a given outcome is stable is $${{\textsf{N}}}{{\textsf{P}}}$$ <mml:math xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"> <mml:mrow> <mml:mi>N</mml:mi> <mml:mi>P</mml:mi> </mml:mrow> </mml:math> -hard in trading networks.
papers.summary_en: Trading networks — multi-sided matching markets with bilateral contracts without transferable utility — have been studied under the assumption of "full substitutability" of preferences. This paper asks the computational question: how hard is it to find stable outcomes? Surprising finding: while "trail-stable" outcomes are easy to find (linear time), full stability (immune to deviation by any set of agents) is NP-hard. Even verifying whether a given outcome is stable is computationally hard. The result has implications for real-world matching-market design.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data. Computational-complexity analysis.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian mechanism-design applications with multi-sided contracting (e.g., healthcare networks, student-residence assignment). The paper's hardness result is a limitation to be aware of when designing trading-network markets. Relevant to KRTK's matching-market research. Caveat: worst-case hardness; many real instances are tractable.

papers.title: Coverings and structure of crossing families
A Matroid Approach to Stable Matchings with Lower Quotas
papers.abstract: In 2010, Huang introduced the laminar classified stable matching problem (lcsm for short) that is motivated by academic hiring. This problem is an extension of the well-known hospitals/residents problem in which a hospital has laminar classes of residents and it sets lower and upper bounds on the number of residents that it can hire in each class. Against the intuition that variations of the stable matching problem with lower quotas are difficult in general, Huang proved that lcsm can be solved in polynomial time. In this paper, we present a matroid-based approach to lcsm and we obtain the following results. (i) We solve a generalization of lcsm in which both sides have quotas. (ii) Huang raised a question about a polyhedral description of the set of stable assignments in lcsm. We give a positive answer for this question by exhibiting a polyhedral description of the set of stable assignments in a generalization of lcsm. (iii) We prove that the set of stable assignments in a generalization of lcsm has a lattice structure that is similar to the (ordinary) stable matching problem.
papers.summary_en: Matching problems often have lower quotas (minimum numbers) for certain categories — like academic hiring requiring at least some junior faculty. Huang (2010) showed this "laminar classified stable matching" problem is polynomial-time solvable despite lower quotas usually making things hard. This paper presents a matroid-based approach and extends Huang's result to (i) generalisations where both sides have quotas and (ii) polyhedral descriptions of stable matchings under these conditions. A significant theoretical extension of a practically important matching problem.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Foundational for Hungarian matching-market design with lower quotas (academic hiring, university admissions with diversity quotas, hospital-doctor matching with regional caps). Relevant to Bíró-group KRTK market-design work. Caveat: theoretical; operational use requires problem-specific tuning.

papers.title: THE NUCLEOLUS OF THE BANKRUPTCY PROBLEM BY HYDRAULIC RATIONING
papers.abstract: In this note, we give a straightforward and elementary proof of a theorem by Aumann and Maschler stating that in the well-known bankruptcy problem, the so-called CG-consistent solution described by the Talmud represents the nucleolus of the corresponding coalitional game. The proof nicely fits into the hydraulic rationing framework proposed by Kaminski. We point out further interesting properties in connection with this framework.

papers.title: COMPENSATION FOR QUALITY DIFFERENCE IN A SEARCH MODEL OF MONEY*
papers.abstract: We study an economy in which there is always double coincidence of wants, agents have perfect information about qualities of goods, and there are no transaction costs. The hold‐up problem arises because efforts invested in improving quality prior to search may not be compensated in the market. Situations in which barter fails to motivate quality improvement are identified. With money, however, the extra effort in quality improvement will be compensated when high‐quality good producers trade with agents holding both the low‐quality good and money. Injection of money can induce almost all agents to produce the high‐quality good.
papers.summary_en: Why does money emerge in economies even when barter is feasible? Fong and Szentes offer an answer rooted in incentive provision for unobservable quality investment. They study an economy with double coincidence of wants, perfect quality information, and no transaction costs — conditions where standard search theories say money is unnecessary. But the hold-up problem still arises: effort invested in improving quality before search may not be compensated under barter. They identify situations where barter fails to motivate quality improvement, and show that injecting money compensates the high-quality producer when high-quality good producers trade with agents holding both the low-quality good and money. With money, almost all agents produce high-quality goods.
papers.summary_hu: Miért alakul ki pénz a gazdaságokban akkor is, ha a csere lehetséges? Fong és Szentes válasza a megfigyelhetetlen minőségi beruházás ösztönzésében gyökerezik. Olyan gazdaságot vizsgálnak, ahol kettős igénykívánalom-egybeesés van, tökéletes minőségi információ és nincs tranzakciós költség — olyan feltételek, ahol a standard kereséselméletek szerint a pénz felesleges. A 'hold-up' probléma azonban így is felmerül: a keresés előtt befektetett minőségjavító erőfeszítés cserénél nem feltétlenül térül meg. A szerzők azonosítják azokat a helyzeteket, amelyekben a csere nem motiválja a minőségjavítást, és megmutatják, hogy a pénz befecskendezése kompenzálja a magas-minőségű termelőt, amikor a magas-minőségű jó termelők alacsony-minőségű jót és pénzt birtokló ágensekkel kereskednek. Pénzzel szinte minden ágens magas-minőségű jót termel.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data. Search-and-matching model of monetary exchange with endogenous quality investment.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; empirikus adatok nélkül. A monetáris csere keresési-illesztési modellje endogén minőségi beruházással.
papers.policy_relevance: Foundational result for monetary-economics theory. Less direct policy relevance, but the paper helps explain why monetary economies tend to have higher quality goods than barter economies, with implications for digital-currency design (where transaction frictions are intentionally low, but quality-investment incentives still need a monetary unit). Caveat: theoretical; specific policy claims require modelling specific exchange technologies.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Alapozó eredmény a monetáris-közgazdasági elméletben. Kevésbé közvetlen szakpolitikai relevancia, de a tanulmány segít megmagyarázni, miért tendálnak a monetáris gazdaságok magasabb minőségű javakra, mint a csere-gazdaságok — implikációkkal a digitális-pénznem tervezéshez (ahol a tranzakciós súrlódások szándékosan alacsonyak, de a minőség-beruházási ösztönzők még mindig monetáris egységet igényelnek). Korlát: elméleti; konkrét szakpolitikai állítások konkrét csere-technológiák modellezését igénylik.

papers.title: Don’t hatch the messenger? On the desirability of restricting the political activity of bureaucrats
papers.abstract: Many countries place restrictions on the political rights of government workers. This includes limitations on political activities such as taking an active part in political campaigns. Are such restrictions desirable? We present a formal welfare analysis of this question. Bureaucrats’ political activities affect voter perceptions of the government and this can have informational benefits. However, they can also induce policy mistakes and are susceptible to ‘noise’ from some bureaucrats’ innate desire for political expression. When politicians have limited control over bureaucrats and successfully coordinate with voters, bureaucrats’ political activities can be desirable. In most cases, however, banning political activities is optimal.
papers.summary_en: Many countries restrict the political activity of civil servants (no campaigning, no party affiliation). Are such restrictions welfare-improving? This paper provides a formal welfare analysis. Bureaucrats' political activities can inform voters about the government — a benefit. But they can also induce policy mistakes and are susceptible to "noise" from bureaucrats' innate political preferences. Result: when politicians have limited control over bureaucrats and voters coordinate well, political activities can be desirable. In most cases, however, banning them is optimal. A theoretical foundation for common civil-service restrictions.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian civil-service legislation (BM, Ministry of Public Administration). The paper supports the general principle of restricting bureaucrats' political activity while acknowledging specific settings where activity could be beneficial. Useful framework for civil-service-reform debates. Caveat: theoretical; specific Hungarian institutional context (political-appointment structures, voter-information flows) would shape the concrete application.

papers.title: Bureaucrats and Policies in Equilibrium Administrations
papers.journal: Journal of the European Economic Association
papers.abstract: Abstract We develop a model of policy making with an endogenous bureaucracy. Parties choose platforms and ideologically differentiated citizens decide whether to enter the public sector, anticipating the platforms that they may be asked to implement. Bureaucrats prefer to work on policies closer to their ideal, and voters judge the performance of an administration taking both politicians’ and bureaucrats’ actions into account. The model provides an equilibrium framework to study the emergence of partisan or neutral bureaucracies and their consequences for government performance. It shows how bureaucratic partisanship can develop in modern civil service systems; why political polarization and bureaucratic partisanship reinforce each other; why bureaucratic neutrality is associated with competitive elections; and why partisanship lowers government efficiency and increases output fluctuations. Our results yield a number of policy implications regarding political appointments, public sector wages, seniority benefits, and recruiting measures that raise the intrinsic motivation of bureaucrats.
papers.summary_en: Why are some civil services neutral and professional, while others are openly partisan? This paper builds a model where parties choose platforms, ideologically-motivated citizens decide whether to work in the public sector, and voters evaluate the resulting administration. The model explains several stylised facts: partisan bureaucracies emerge under polarised politics; neutral bureaucracies are associated with competitive elections; partisan civil services lower government efficiency and produce larger swings in policy output. It yields predictions for when political appointments should be extensive versus limited, and for how civil-service rules interact with electoral competition.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Very direct for Hungary. The model links political polarisation, bureaucratic partisanship, and government efficiency — a frame that maps onto ongoing debates about Hungarian civil-service reform, political appointments, and the tenure security of senior officials. Relevant to MTA/HUN-REN governance debates, public-administration reform discussions, and to any future civil-service law revision. Caveat: the model is stylised; applying it to a specific country requires mapping its parameters (polarisation, monitoring technology) to local institutions, which is an empirical project not undertaken in the paper.

papers.title: Preference Erosion and Multilateral Trade Liberalization
papers.abstract: Because of concern that tariff reductions in Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) countries will translate into worsening export performance for the least developed countries, the erosion of trade preferences may become a stumbling block for multilateral trade liberalization. An econometric analysis of actual preference use shows that preferences are underused because of administrative burdens--estimated to be equivalent to an average of 4 percent of the value of goods traded. To quantify the maximum scope for preference erosion, the compliance cost estimates are used in a model-based assessment of the impact of full elimination of OECD tariffs. Taking into account administrative costs eliminates erosion costs in the aggregate and greatly reduces the losses for countries most affected by preference erosion.
papers.summary_en: Will OECD tariff reductions hurt the least-developed countries by eroding the trade preferences they currently enjoy? François, Hoekman and Manchin look at actual preference utilisation by African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) countries exporting to the OECD. They find preferences are systematically underused because of administrative compliance burdens — equivalent to about 4% of the value of goods traded. Using these compliance-cost estimates, they quantify the maximum scope for preference erosion: it is far smaller than gross-tariff calculations suggest. The empirical result tempers worries about preference erosion as a stumbling block for multilateral trade liberalisation; the policy lever is administrative simplification, not preserving tariff margins.
papers.summary_hu: Vajon az OECD-vámcsökkentések ártanak-e a legkevésbé fejlett országoknak azzal, hogy erodálják a jelenleg élvezett kereskedelmi kedvezményeiket? François, Hoekman és Manchin a tényleges kedvezmény-felhasználást vizsgálja afrikai, karibi és csendes-óceáni (ACP) országok OECD-be irányuló exportjában. A kedvezményeket szisztematikusan alulhasználják az adminisztratív megfelelési terhek miatt — ezek a kereskedelmi javak értékének átlagosan 4 százalékával egyenértékűek. Ezekkel a megfelelési költség-becslésekkel kvantifikálják a kedvezmény-erózió maximális mértékét: ez sokkal kisebb, mint amit a bruttó-vám számítások sugallnak. Az empirikus eredmény tompítja a kedvezmény-erózióval kapcsolatos aggályokat, mint a multilaterális kereskedelmi liberalizáció akadálya; a szakpolitikai eszköz az adminisztratív egyszerűsítés, nem a vámmarzsok megőrzése.
papers.data_used: EU and other OECD import-tariff and preference-utilisation data, late 1990s to mid-2000s, by HS chapter and ACP partner. Compliance costs estimated from gap between potential and actual utilisation rates.
papers.data_used_hu: EU és más OECD import-vám- és kedvezmény-felhasználási adatok, 1990-es évek vége — 2000-es évek közepe, HS-fejezet és ACP-partner szerint. A megfelelési költségeket a potenciális és tényleges felhasználási ráták közötti rés alapján becslik.
papers.policy_relevance: Useful for Hungarian Mission to the EU and ITM trade policy on EU Generalised System of Preferences (GSP) reforms. The paper's key finding — that compliance burden is the binding constraint, not the headline tariff — argues for prioritising rules-of-origin simplification and administrative-cost reduction in preference programmes. The same logic applies to Hungarian-specific export-promotion programmes that involve burdensome certification.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Hasznos a Magyar Misszió az EU-nál és az ITM EU Általános Vámpreferencia-rendszer (GSP) reformokkal kapcsolatos kereskedelmi politikájához. A tanulmány kulcsmegállapítása — hogy a megfelelési teher a kötelező korlát, nem a vám — a származási szabályok egyszerűsítését és az adminisztratív költségek csökkentését támogatja a kedvezményprogramokban. Ugyanez a logika alkalmazható azokra a magyar-specifikus exportösztönzési programokra is, amelyek bonyolult tanúsítást igényelnek.

papers.title: Institutions, Infrastructure, and Trade
Protection and Performance
papers.abstract: Abstract We examine the linkages between import policy and export performance, extending classic macroeconomic trade effects to more recent concepts from the modern literature on gravity models. We also examine these effects empirically with a panel of global and bilateral trade spanning 15 years. Our emphasis on the role of import policy (i.e. tariffs) of exporters as an explanation of trade volumes contrasts with the recent emphasis on importer policy in the gravity literature. It also reinforces the growing body of evidence on the importance of economic environmental (policy and infrastructure) conditions in explaining relative export performance and is in line with the literature on global value chains.
papers.summary_en: Does a country's import policy affect its export performance? François and Manchin examine the link between exporters' own tariffs and their export volumes, extending the modern gravity literature beyond its usual focus on importer-side policy. Using a 15-year panel of bilateral trade flows, they show that an exporter's own protection (tariffs on its own imports) materially worsens its export performance. The mechanism is that exporters are firms in tradeable sectors that need imported intermediates; high own-tariffs raise their costs. The result reinforces broader evidence on the role of trade-policy environment in shaping trade volumes — and shifts emphasis from importer to exporter policy.
papers.summary_hu: Befolyásolja-e egy ország importpolitikája az exportteljesítményét? François és Manchin az exportőrök saját vámjai és az exportvolumenek közötti kapcsolatot vizsgálja, kiterjesztve a modern gravitációs irodalmat annak szokásos importőr-oldali fókuszán túlra. 15 éves kétoldalú kereskedelmi panellel megmutatják, hogy egy exportőr saját védettsége (saját importjára kivetett vámok) érdemben rontja exportteljesítményét. A mechanizmus szerint az exportőrök forgalmazható szektorokban működő cégek, amelyeknek importált intermedier inputokra van szükségük; a magas saját vámok növelik a költségeiket. Az eredmény megerősíti a kereskedelmi-politikai környezet kereskedelmi volumeneket alakító szerepéről szóló szélesebb bizonyítékokat — és az importőri szakpolitikáról az exportőri szakpolitikára helyezi a hangsúlyt.
papers.data_used: Global panel of bilateral trade flows over 15 years (~1995–2010), with both exporter and importer tariff data. Standard gravity specification augmented with exporter own-tariff variable.
papers.data_used_hu: Globális kétoldalú kereskedelmi áramlások panelje 15 évre (~1995–2010), exportőri és importőri vámadatokkal egyaránt. Standard gravitációs specifikáció exportőri saját-vám változóval kibővítve.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian trade-policy debates: argues that maintaining low tariffs on Hungary's own intermediate-input imports is critical for export competitiveness, even before considering importer-side market access. NGM and the Foreign Affairs Ministry should prioritise import-side liberalisation in trade negotiations on the same level as export-market access. Particularly relevant for ongoing CETA / future US-EU TTIP-style negotiations where reciprocal tariff reductions are the main lever.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: A magyar kereskedelmi-politikai vitákhoz: érvel amellett, hogy az export-versenyképességhez a magyar saját intermedier-import alacsony vámjainak fenntartása kritikus, még mielőtt az importőri-oldali piaci hozzáférést megfontolnánk. Az NGM-nek és a Külügyminisztériumnak a kereskedelmi tárgyalásokon az import-oldali liberalizációt ugyanolyan szinten kell priorizálnia, mint az exportpiaci hozzáférést. Különösen releváns a folyamatban lévő CETA / jövőbeli US-EU TTIP-szerű tárgyalások számára, ahol a kölcsönös vámcsökkentések a fő eszköz.

papers.title: Market power in distribution and pass‐through for consumers and producers
papers.abstract: Abstract We examine the differential pass‐through of import prices into consumer and producer prices. We develop a framework with distribution costs and distribution market power. We then examine pass‐through from import prices to consumer and producer prices in the euro area using the U.S. import price as instrument. We find that pass‐through rates to producer prices are more sensitive to changes in distribution margins than pass‐through to consumer prices. Furthermore, only a portion of import price changes translate into domestic price changes limiting potential consumer benefits from tariff liberalization, with market power in distribution services being one important factor reducing pass‐through.
papers.summary_en: When import prices change, how much does that translate into consumer and producer prices? This paper develops a framework with distribution costs and distribution market power to study the differential pass-through. Using euro-area data and US import price as instrument, the authors find: pass-through to producer prices is more sensitive to distribution margins than pass-through to consumer prices; only a portion of import-price changes reach domestic prices. Market power in distribution services is an important factor reducing consumer benefits from tariff liberalization.
papers.data_used: Euro-area import, consumer, and producer price data. US import price used as instrument for euro-area import prices.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian inflation analysis and trade-policy evaluation (MNB, ITM). The paper shows that tariff cuts and import-price changes don't pass through fully to Hungarian consumers — distribution-sector market power intercepts part of the benefit. Relevant to competition policy for retail distribution (GVH) and to trade-policy impact-assessment. Caveat: euro-area data; Hungarian-specific distribution-margin dynamics differ.

papers.title: Regional Integration in Asia: The Role of Infrastructure
papers.abstract: Abstract The rapid growth of intra-Asian trade, especially in the last couple of decades, represents both a marked shift in export orientation and an increasing integration of regional markets. In 2005, half of total Asian exports were destined for regional consumption, and a further rise can only be expected given the high growth rates of intraregional trade, peaking at 25% in 2004 (World Trade Organization [WTO] 2006). Against such a backdrop, the surge of regionalism in Asia today comes as no surprise. Nowhere in the world is the proliferation of regional integration arrangements (RIAs) more evident than in Asia today. As of September 2006, the individual East and South Asian countries considered in this study were involved in 301 free trade negotiations or signed agreements. While political and security motives are also influential, the economic imperative to further feed the market’s appetite for more regional trade is clearly driving policy agents along the track toward free trade agreements (FTAs).
papers.summary_en: Why did intra-Asian trade grow so fast through the 2000s, and what role did infrastructure play? François, Manchin and Pelkmans-Balaoing analyse rapid Asian regional integration: in 2005, half of total Asian exports were intra-regional, and intraregional trade growth peaked at 25% in 2004. The proliferation of Regional Integration Arrangements (RIAs) is partly explained by trade-friendly policy, but the chapter's core finding is that physical infrastructure — ports, road and rail capacity, customs efficiency — constitutes the binding constraint on further trade growth in many parts of Asia, even more than tariff reductions. Future growth will depend on infrastructure investment.
papers.summary_hu: Miért nőtt olyan gyorsan az ázsián belüli kereskedelem a 2000-es években, és milyen szerepet játszott az infrastruktúra? François, Manchin és Pelkmans-Balaoing a gyors ázsiai regionális integrációt elemzi: 2005-ben az ázsiai export fele régión belüli volt, és az ezen belüli növekedés 2004-ben 25 százalékkal csúcsosodott. A Regionális Integrációs Megállapodások (RIA-k) elterjedése részben kereskedelem-barát szakpolitikával magyarázható, de a fejezet kulcsmegállapítása szerint a fizikai infrastruktúra — kikötők, közúti és vasúti kapacitás, vámhivatali hatékonyság — sok ázsiai területen a további kereskedelem-növekedés kötelező korlátja, még a vámcsökkentéseknél is jobban. A jövőbeli növekedés az infrastruktúra-beruházástól függ.
papers.data_used: Asian intra-regional trade flow data, 1990s–2008. Combined with infrastructure-quality measures (port and road indicators, customs-time benchmarks). Gravity-style modelling with infrastructure terms.
papers.data_used_hu: Ázsián belüli kereskedelmi áramlási adatok, 1990-es évek – 2008. Infrastruktúra-minőségi mértékekkel (kikötő- és úti mutatók, vámidő-benchmarkok) kombinálva. Gravitáció-stílusú modellezés infrastruktúra-tagokkal.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian foreign-policy and ITM strategy: a useful comparison framework when assessing the role of physical-infrastructure investment in V4 + Three Seas Initiative trade growth. The Asian experience suggests that infrastructure spending complements tariff harmonisation, not just substitutes for it. For Hungarian export-route diversification (toward Asia, Middle East), the paper highlights port-and-rail bottlenecks as the binding constraint to address.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: A magyar külpolitika és ITM-stratégia szempontjából: hasznos összehasonlító keret a V4 + Három Tenger Kezdeményezés kereskedelmi növekedésében a fizikai-infrastruktúra beruházás szerepének értékeléséhez. Az ázsiai tapasztalat szerint az infrastruktúra-kiadás kiegészíti a vámharmonizációt, nem csak helyettesíti. A magyar export-útvonalak diverzifikációjához (Ázsia, Közel-Kelet felé) a tanulmány a kikötő- és vasúti szűk keresztmetszeteket emeli ki kötelező korlátként.

papers.title: Market Structure in Multisector General Equilibrium Models of Open Economies
papers.journal: Handbook of computable general equilibrium modeling

papers.title: A Study of Bankruptcy Costs and the Allocation of Control
papers.abstract: Abstract This article studies how the allocation of control rights in bankruptcy influences outcomes. Using Hungarian data, we find that the large majority of bankrupt firms in our sample are maintained as going concerns despite the fact that these firms generate large operating losses and low recovery rates for pre-bankruptcy creditors. We trace the bias to the allocation of control rights between secured and unsecured creditors and the compensation scheme of the agent managing the bankruptcy process. Our findings shed light on a very important bankruptcy design question: how particular provisions of the bankruptcy code affect the costs of bankruptcy and the size of the pie available to pre-bankruptcy creditors.

papers.title: Kyoto Protocol cooperation: Does government corruption facilitate environmental lobbying?
Regulating financial conglomerates
International migration and illegal costs: Evidence from Africa-to-Europe smuggling routes
papers.abstract: The 2011 Arab Spring marked the opening of the Central Mediterranean Route for irregular border crossings between Libya and Italy, which produced heterogeneous reductions of bilateral smuggling distances between country pairs in the Mediterranean region. We exploit this source of spatial and temporal variation in bilateral distance along land and sea routes to estimate the elasticity of irregular migration intentions for African and Near East countries. We estimate an elasticity of migration intentions to smuggling distances exceeding -3, mainly driven by countries with weak rule of law and high internet penetration. Our findings are consistent across irregular migration measures both at the aggregate and individual levels. We show that irregular migration elasticity is higher for youth, relatively skilled individuals and those with an informative advantage (having a social network abroad or a mobile phone).
papers.summary_en: How does smuggling cost shape migration flows? Exploiting the opening of the Central Mediterranean route after the 2011 Arab Spring — which reduced smuggling distances unevenly across country pairs — this paper estimates the elasticity of irregular migration intentions to smuggling distance. The elasticity exceeds -3 and is concentrated in countries with weak rule of law and high internet penetration. Youth, skilled individuals, and those with a mobile phone or social network abroad are most responsive. The paper is one of the cleanest quantifications of smuggling-cost effects on migration in the literature.
papers.summary_hu: Hogyan alakítja a csempészköltség a migrációs áramlásokat? A tanulmány kihasználja a Központi Mediterrán útvonal 2011-es arab tavaszt követő megnyílását — amely egyenetlenül csökkentette a csempészési távolságokat ország-párok között — és megbecsli az irreguláris migrációs szándékok csempészési-távolságra vonatkozó rugalmasságát. A rugalmasság meghaladja a -3-at, és olyan országokra koncentrálódik, ahol a jogállamiság gyenge és az internet-penetráció magas. A fiatalok, képzett egyének és mobiltelefonnal vagy külföldi szociális hálózattal rendelkezők a legérzékenyebbek. A csempészköltség-hatások egyik legtisztább kvantifikálása a szakirodalomban.
papers.data_used: Gallup World Poll migration-intention data from African and Near East countries, 2008–2019; combined with georeferenced smuggling routes and exogenous distance shocks from the 2011 opening of the Central Mediterranean Route.
papers.data_used_hu: Gallup World Poll migrációs-szándék adatok afrikai és közel-keleti országokból, 2008–2019; georeferenciált csempészési útvonalakkal és a Központi Mediterrán útvonal 2011-es megnyílásából származó exogén távolsági sokkokkal kombinálva.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to EU migration policy debates, in which Hungary has been a central voice. For the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Ministry of Interior: the paper's large elasticity means that marginal changes in border infrastructure or smuggling enforcement have disproportionate effects on migration flows — a double-edged result for policy. Also relevant for understanding which African countries are the main source of irregular migration and how internet penetration amplifies responsiveness to route openings.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlenül releváns az EU migrációs politikai vitáihoz, amelyekben Magyarország központi szereplő volt. A Külügyminisztériumnak és a Belügyminisztériumnak: a tanulmány nagy rugalmassága azt jelenti, hogy a határinfrastruktúra vagy a csempészek elleni fellépés marginális változásainak aránytalan hatása van a migrációs áramlásokra — kétélű eredmény a politika számára. Releváns annak megértéséhez is, mely afrikai országok a fő irreguláris-migrációs források, és hogyan erősíti az internet-penetráció az útvonal-megnyílásokra való reagálást.

papers.title: Better luck next time: Learning through retaking
Employment and Poverty Dynamics Before, During, and After the Crisis
papers.abstract: Abstract This chapter investigates how changes in employment and poverty relate to each other across the European Union’s Member States. Large employment volatility was accompanied by sizable changes in poverty rates between 2005 and 2012. Based on panel regression results, the poverty to employment elasticity was estimated to be around 25% on average. The role of changes in the poverty rates of individuals in jobless and non-jobless households and of changes in the share of those in jobless households differs greatly across countries. The success of poverty reduction depends to a large extent on three factors: the dynamics of overall employment growth, the fair distribution of employment growth across households with different levels of work intensity, and properly designed social welfare systems to smooth out income losses for families in need.

papers.title: Unravelling the relationship between employment, social transfers and income poverty
The effects of child-related benefits and pensions on fertility by birth order: A test on Hungarian data
papers.abstract: Using aggregate time-series data from post-war Hungary, we investigated the effect of child-related benefits and pensions on overall fertility and fertility by birth order. The results indicate moderate effects that are robust across a wide range of specifications. According to our estimates, a 1-per-cent increase in child-related benefits would increase total fertility by 0.2 per cent, while the same increase in pensions would decrease fertility by 0.2 per cent. The magnitude of both effects increases by birth order; this is more robust for child-related benefits.
papers.summary_en: Does paying families more for each child actually raise birth rates? This paper uses Hungarian post-war aggregate time series to estimate the effect of child-related cash benefits and pensions on fertility by birth order. The effects are moderate and consistent: a 1% rise in child-related benefits raises total fertility by about 0.2%, while a 1% rise in pension benefits lowers it by 0.2%. The magnitudes grow with birth order — most policy response comes from second and later births — and are more precisely estimated for child-related benefits than for pensions.
papers.summary_hu: Valóban emeli-e a gyermekvállalási hajlandóságot, ha a családok több támogatást kapnak gyermekenként? A tanulmány magyar háború utáni aggregált idősorokat használva megbecsüli a gyermekekhez kötött készpénzes juttatások és a nyugdíjak hatását a születési sorrend szerinti termékenységre. A hatások mérsékeltek és következetesek: a gyermekekhez kötött juttatások 1%-os emelkedése a teljes termékenységet körülbelül 0,2%-kal emeli, míg a nyugdíjak 1%-os emelkedése 0,2%-kal csökkenti. A nagyságok nőnek a születési sorrend szerint — a szakpolitikai válasz nagy része a második és későbbi születésekből jön —, és pontosabban becsülhetők a gyermek-juttatásoknál, mint a nyugdíjaknál.
papers.data_used: Hungarian aggregate time series, 1950–2006: fertility rates by birth order, child-related family benefits (child allowance, GYES, GYED), and pension benefits, from KSH and the Ministry of Finance.
papers.data_used_hu: Magyar aggregált idősorok, 1950–2006: születési-sorrend szerinti termékenységi ráták, gyermekekhez kötött családi juttatások (családi pótlék, GYES, GYED) és nyugdíjak a KSH-tól és a Pénzügyminisztériumtól.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian family-policy design (Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Interior family-portfolio). The estimates suggest that large increases in family benefits are needed to move fertility much: a 10% rise lifts TFR by only 2%. Pension policy cuts the other way: raising pension replacement rates reduces fertility, which matters when pension reforms and fertility targets are designed jointly. Uses data through 2006, excluding the 2010s CSOK-era policy mix; newer research is needed for the most recent period.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlenül releváns a magyar családpolitikához (Pénzügyminisztérium, Belügyminisztérium családi portfóliója). A becslések szerint a családi juttatások nagy emeléseire van szükség ahhoz, hogy a termékenységet jelentősen elmozdítsák: 10%-os emelés csak 2%-kal emeli a TFR-t. A nyugdíjpolitika ellenkező irányba hat: a nyugdíjpótlási ráták emelése csökkenti a termékenységet, ami fontos, amikor nyugdíjreformokat és termékenységi célokat közösen tervezünk. 2006-ig használ adatokat, kizárva a 2010-es évek CSOK-korszakbeli szakpolitikai mixet; újabb kutatás kell a legújabb időszakra.

papers.title: Market outcomes and dynamic patent buyouts
A theory of grand innovation prizes
MARKET POWER AND EFFICIENCY IN A SEARCH MODEL*
papers.abstract: We build a theoretical model to study the welfare effects and policy implications of firms’ market power in a frictional labor market. The main characteristics of our environment are that wages play a role in allocating labor across firms and the number of agents is finite. The decentralized equilibrium is inefficient and the firms’ market power results in the misallocation of workers from the high to the low productivity firms. A minimum wage exacerbates the inefficiencies by forcing the low-productivity firms to increase their wage. Moderate unemployment benefits can increase welfare by improving the workers’ outside option.
papers.summary_en: Classical labour search models assume wages are determined by bargaining. But if firms post wages to compete for workers, how do market power and inefficiency interact? This paper builds a search model where wages play a role in allocating workers across firms and there's a finite number of agents. The decentralised equilibrium is inefficient: firms with market power misallocate workers from high-productivity to low-productivity firms. A binding minimum wage makes things worse by forcing low-productivity firms up. Moderate unemployment benefits can improve welfare by strengthening workers' outside option. Nuanced implications for labour-market policy.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Direct relevance to the minimum-wage debate and unemployment-benefit generosity in Hungary. The paper supports the view that minimum wages can reduce efficiency by compressing market-allocative wage differentials — but also supports moderate UI generosity as a complementary tool. For the Hungarian Ministry of Economy and VKF social dialogue on wage floors, the paper offers a balanced theoretical framing. Caveat: theoretical; the model omits some realistic frictions (union bargaining, industry-specific wage floors) relevant in Hungary.

papers.title: Local interaction in tax evasion
Optimal technology design
Corruption and Extremism
Újabb hozzászólás az ismét elmaradt minimálbérvitához
papers.abstract: Ebben a tanulmányban a számunkra elérhető legfrissebb, 2021. májusi járulékfizetési adatok alapján következtettünk a 2022. évi minimálbér-emelés várható hatásaira. Az adatok alapján úgy sejtjük, hogy a minimálbér-emelés főként a kisvállalatokat és a szakképzettséget nem igénylő munkaköröket érinti, így az aggregált adatok vizsgálata nem elegendő a minimálbér-emelés hatásainak vizsgálatához. Kutatásunk alapján nem látjuk jelét, hogy a minimálbér melletti zsebbe fizetés széles körű jelenség lenne. Végül a minimálbér adóterhének csökkentése mellett érvelünk.
papers.summary_en: What will the 2022 Hungarian minimum-wage hike do? Gáspár and Reizer use the latest available contribution-payment data (May 2021) to forecast the effects of the announced increase. Their analysis flags two findings. First, the hike disproportionately affects small firms and unskilled-occupation jobs, so aggregate-statistics-based assessments will miss the true distributional impact. Second, they find no evidence that 'envelope wages' (cash payments above the minimum, off the books) are widespread — meaning the headline minimum-wage rise will translate fairly directly into reported labour costs for affected firms. They argue for cutting the tax wedge on the minimum wage as the appropriate complementary policy.
papers.summary_hu: Mit fog tenni a 2022-es magyar minimálbér-emelés? Gáspár és Reizer a legfrissebb elérhető járulékfizetési adatok (2021 május) alapján vetít a bejelentett emelés hatásaira. Az elemzés két megállapítást emel ki. Először, az emelés aránytalanul érinti a kisvállalatokat és a szakképzettséget nem igénylő munkaköröket, így az aggregált statisztikák nem mutatják meg a valódi eloszlási hatást. Másodszor, nem találnak bizonyítékot arra, hogy a 'borítékos' bérezés (a minimum feletti bejelentetlen készpénzes fizetés) széles körű lenne — vagyis a bejelentett minimálbér-emelés tisztességesen átfordul az érintett cégek bejelentett munkaerőköltségében. A minimálbér adóterhének csökkentését javasolják kiegészítő szakpolitikaként.
papers.data_used: Hungarian administrative firm-level contribution-payment data, May 2021. Allows decomposition by firm size, sector, and occupation skill level. Static micro-simulation of the 2022 minimum-wage hike effect.
papers.data_used_hu: Magyar adminisztratív cégszintű járulékfizetési adatok, 2021. május. Lehetővé teszi a felbontást cégméret, ágazat és foglalkozási képzettségi szint szerint. A 2022-es minimálbér-emelés hatásának statikus mikroszimulációja.
papers.policy_relevance: Direct policy input for NGM and the Pénzügyminisztérium on minimum-wage strategy. Two policy levers identified: (i) keep the minimum wage cuts targeted to small-firm sectors where the bite is largest, and (ii) reduce the tax wedge on the minimum wage rather than only adjusting the gross level. The 'envelope wage is rare' finding affects how to interpret current minimum-wage compliance — argues against assuming widespread evasion when calibrating audit budgets.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlen szakpolitikai input az NGM és a Pénzügyminisztérium minimálbér-stratégiájához. Két szakpolitikai eszköz azonosítva: (i) a minimálbér-csökkentések maradjanak célzottak a kisvállalati ágazatokra, ahol a hatás a legnagyobb, és (ii) inkább a minimálbér adóterhét csökkentsük, mint csupán a bruttó szintet módosítsuk. A 'borítékos bér ritka' megállapítás befolyásolja, hogy hogyan értelmezzük a jelenlegi minimálbér-megfelelést — érvel az ellenőrzési költségvetés kalibrálásakor a széles körű adóelkerülés feltételezése ellen.

papers.title: Prioritization vs zero-rating: Discrimination on the internet
Flexible Moral Hazard Problems
papers.abstract: This paper considers a moral hazard problem where the agent can choose any output distribution with a support in a given compact set.
