Data that can identify women and men who do the same work for the same employer are rare, and research informing this crucial aspect of gender differences in pay is several decades old and from a limited number of countries. Here, using recent linked employer-employee data from 15 countries, we show that the processes sorting people into different jobs account for substantially less of the gender pay differences than was previously believed and that within-job pay differences remain consequential.
papers.summary_en: The standard story about the gender pay gap has been: within the same job, men and women are paid similarly; the gap comes from sorting women into lower-paying jobs. This paper uses recent linked employer-employee data from 15 countries to challenge that story. The finding: job sorting accounts for less of the gap than previously believed, and within-job pay differences remain substantial. Policy implications flip — equal-pay enforcement (within-job) matters more than previously thought. An empirical update that should reshape how governments and firms design gender-equality policies.
papers.data_used: Linked employer-employee administrative data from 15 countries (including Hungary, via Boza and Hajdu). Covers within-job versus between-job pay decomposition.
papers.policy_relevance: Central for Hungarian gender-equality policy (ETDA, Ministry of Labour). The paper supports strengthened within-job pay-transparency and equal-pay-enforcement mechanisms, including audits of pay bands within firms. Relevant to implementation of the EU Pay Transparency Directive. Caveat: countries differ; Hungarian magnitudes are embedded in the sample via Hungarian data contributors, but full domestic estimates with more recent micro-data would be complementary.

papers.title: Definable and Contractible Contracts
papers.abstract: This paper analyzes Bayesian normal form games in which players write contracts that condition their actions on the contracts of other players. These contracts are required to be representable in a formal language. This is accomplished by constructing contracts which are definable functions of the Godel code of every other player's contract. We provide a complete characterization of the set of allocations supportable as pure-strategy Bayesian equilibria of this contracting game. When information is complete, this characterization provides a folk theorem. In general, the set of supportable allocations is smaller than the set supportable by a centralized mechanism designer.
papers.summary_en: In many real strategic settings, players write contracts that condition their own behaviour on what other players are doing — essentially, contracts about contracts. This paper makes that idea mathematically precise: it requires contracts to be writable in a formal language, using Gödel codes of other players' contracts. The authors fully characterise which allocations can be supported as pure-strategy Bayesian equilibria of this "contracting game." Under complete information, they establish a folk theorem. In general, fewer allocations are supportable than under a centralised mechanism designer. A foundational technical contribution to mechanism design.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Primarily academic. The decentralised contracting framework underlies analysis of smart-contract networks, blockchain coordination, and self-enforcing agreements — increasingly policy-relevant areas. Caveat: the result is abstract; implementation in specific Hungarian regulatory contexts (e.g., Web3 policy) requires domain-specific mapping.

papers.title: Gender differences in the skill content of jobs
papers.abstract: Abstract There is significant heterogeneity in actual skill use within occupations even though occupations are differentiated by the task workers should perform during work. Using data on 12 countries which are available both in the Programme for the International Assessment of Adult Competencies survey and International Social Survey Program, we show that women use their cognitive skills less than men even within the same occupation. The gap in skill intensity cannot be explained by differences in worker characteristics or in cognitive skills. Instead, we show that living in a partnership significantly increases the skill use of men compared with women. We argue that having a partner affects skill use through time allocation as the gender penalty of partnered women is halved once we control for working hours and hours spent on housework. Finally, we do not find evidence of workplace discrimination against women.
papers.summary_en: Within the same occupation, do men and women use cognitive skills differently? Pető and Reizer combine PIAAC skill-assessment data with ISSP survey data across 12 countries to compare actual on-the-job skill use between genders. Within an occupation, women use cognitive skills less than men. The gap survives controls for worker characteristics and even for cognitive-skill levels — so it isn't selection or ability. The mechanism appears to be partnership status: living in a partnership raises men's skill use, but lowers women's. Once working hours and housework hours are controlled for, the gender penalty halves — pointing to time allocation as the channel. They find no evidence of workplace discrimination per se.
papers.summary_hu: Ugyanazon a foglalkozáson belül a férfiak és nők különbözőképpen használják kognitív képességeiket? Pető és Reizer a PIAAC készség-értékelési adatokat és az ISSP felméréseket ötvözi 12 országra, hogy összehasonlítsa a tényleges, munkahelyi képesség-használatot nemek szerint. Egy foglalkozáson belül a nők kevesebbet használják kognitív képességeiket, mint a férfiak. A különbség nem múlik el a munkavállaló-jellemzők, sőt a kognitív-képességszintek beépítésével — tehát nem szelekció vagy képességek kérdése. A mechanizmus a párkapcsolati státusz: a párkapcsolatban élés növeli a férfiak képesség-használatát, de csökkenti a nőkét. Amikor a munkaórákat és a háztartási órákat is beépítjük, a nemi büntetés feleződik — ez az időallokációra mint csatornára mutat. Munkahelyi diszkriminációra önmagában nem találnak bizonyítékot.
papers.data_used: PIAAC (Programme for International Assessment of Adult Competencies) on-the-job skill-use measures merged with ISSP (International Social Survey Programme) on housework hours and partnership status. 12 OECD countries; sample size approximately 30,000 working adults.
papers.data_used_hu: PIAAC munkahelyi képesség-használati mutatók ISSP-vel összekapcsolva (háztartási órák, párkapcsolati státusz). 12 OECD-ország; mintegy 30 000 dolgozó felnőtt.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian gender-equality policy and the EBH (Equal Treatment Authority): the paper undermines the 'workplace discrimination' framing of the within-occupation gender skill-use gap and points instead at home time allocation as the binding constraint. Policy implication: childcare provision, housework-redistribution norms, and parental-leave-design are likelier to close the gap than within-firm equality interventions. Useful counterweight to discrimination-only narratives in HR policy debates.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: A magyar nemi egyenlőségi politika és az EBH számára: a tanulmány aláássa a 'munkahelyi diszkrimináció' keretezést a foglalkozáson belüli nemi képesség-használati résre, és helyette az otthoni időallokációra mint kötelező korlátra mutat. Szakpolitikai implikáció: a gyermekgondozási ellátás, a háztartási munka újraosztásának normái és a szülői szabadság tervezése valószínűbben zárja a rést, mint a vállalaton belüli egyenlőségi beavatkozások. Hasznos ellensúly a HR-szakpolitikai vitákban a tisztán diszkrimináció-alapú narratíváknak.

papers.title: Presidential Elections, Divided Politics, and Happiness in the <scp>USA</scp>
papers.abstract: We examine the effects of the 2016 and 2012 US presidential election outcomes on subjective wellbeing across party identification. We use Gallup data and a regression discontinuity approach, and focus primarily on evaluative (life satisfaction) and hedonic (positive and negative affect) indicators. We find that both elections had strong negative wellbeing effects on those who identified with the losing party, with little or no increase in wellbeing for those identifying with the winning party. The negative effects for the losing side were larger in 2016 than in 2012, by a factor of three on some indicators, and were driven mainly by women and middle‐income households. As such, both elections had a net negative wellbeing effect, but more so in 2016. Local voting patterns did not have a substantial wellbeing impact, nor did congressional elections taking place the same day. In 2016, the election also changed respondents’ perceptions about the economy, their financial status, and their community. After both elections, hedonic wellbeing gaps across parties typically dissipated within two weeks, but there was more persistence in evaluative wellbeing gaps, especially in expected life satisfaction. The latter gap persisted throughout 2017.
papers.summary_en: Elections have winners and losers — and the losers feel it. This paper uses Gallup data around the 2012 and 2016 US presidential elections, with a regression-discontinuity around election day, to estimate well-being effects. Finding: losing-party identifiers experience substantial drops in life satisfaction and negative affect, while winning-party identifiers show little gain. Net wellbeing effect of both elections was negative, more so in 2016. Effects concentrated in women and middle-income households. A clean quantitative account of the political-polarisation costs of elections.
papers.data_used: Gallup wellbeing data around 2012 and 2016 US presidential elections. Individual-level party-identification and life-satisfaction measurements. Regression-discontinuity around election day.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian political-economy analysis: the paper documents the wellbeing cost of polarised politics, particularly for the losing side. Relevant to thinking about civic-polarisation indicators and to discussions of electoral-system design. Caveat: US context; Hungarian political-polarisation dynamics are different. Hungary-specific replication would be informative.

papers.title: Gambling by auctions
Employment and Wage Consequences of Flexible Wage Components
papers.abstract: I document new facts about the relationship between flexible wage components and firm performance using a unique matched employer-employee database from Hungary. Firms providing flexible wage components adjust total wage compensation more to revenue shocks than firms without flexible wages. Nevertheless, employment responses to revenue shocks are the same at firms with and without flexible wage components. These findings also hold in the case of aggregate shocks and during the Great Recession. The results suggest that flexible wage components in their current magnitude do not attenuate employment responses to a negative revenue shock. Finally, I discuss the possible explanations for the empirical findings.
papers.summary_en: Hungarian firms often pay flexible bonuses on top of fixed wages. Does this flexibility act as a shock absorber, letting firms avoid layoffs during bad times? This paper uses matched employer-employee data from Hungary to test the idea. Findings are mixed. Firms with flexible wage components do adjust total wage compensation more to revenue shocks than firms without. But employment responses are the same across firm types — flexibility in the wage doesn't shield jobs. This holds during the Great Recession. The conclusion: in their current magnitude, flexible wage components do not attenuate employment responses to negative shocks.
papers.data_used: Matched employer-employee administrative data from Hungary, covering the 2000s through the Great Recession. Individual-level wage, bonus, and employment records linked to firm financials.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian labour-market flexibility debates (VKF social dialogue, Ministry of Finance). Conventional policy wisdom is that flexible wage components create shock-absorption; this paper shows they don't in the Hungarian context. Implications for minimum-wage design, Kurzarbeit-style short-time programmes, and employment-protection policy. Relevant to the Ministry of Economy. Caveat: data cover through 2010s; tighter labour markets post-2015 may have changed the flexibility-employment trade-off.

papers.title: The roots of economic failure: what explains East Germany's falling behind between 1945 and 1950?
papers.abstract: The relative decline of the East German economy after 1945 has eluded researchers, as several large shocks appeared to have hit it at the same time. In this paper, we revisit the immediate post-war period in both parts of Germany to obtain a more comprehensive picture of the output and productivity shocks operating in both economies. Our principal finding is that the dismantling of the capital stock alone cannot explain the inferior performance of the East German economy. The collapse of output after the war and the ensuing recovery in both parts of the country were driven by total factor productivity; changes in factor endowments were of second-order significance. West Germany began to lead East Germany in industrial labor productivity well before the economic reforms of 1948 could make their mark. The major factor contributing to this early divergence were disproportions in industrial structure caused by the division of Germany.
papers.summary_en: Why did East Germany fall behind West Germany so quickly after 1945? The standard explanations focus on the Soviet dismantling of capital stock. Ritschl and Vonyó revisit the immediate post-war period with a fuller accounting of output and productivity shocks in both Germanies. Their main finding: dismantling alone cannot explain the East German underperformance. Both economies' post-war collapse and recovery were driven mainly by total factor productivity, not factor endowments. West Germany began leading East Germany in industrial labour productivity well before the 1948 economic reforms. The major factor causing the early divergence was disproportions in industrial structure caused by Germany's division — not Soviet capital seizures alone.
papers.summary_hu: Miért szakadt le Kelet-Németország Nyugat-Németországtól olyan gyorsan 1945 után? A standard magyarázatok a tőkeállomány szovjet leszerelésére fókuszálnak. Ritschl és Vonyó újra megvizsgálja a közvetlen háború utáni időszakot mindkét Németország kibocsátási és termelékenységi sokkjainak teljesebb elszámolásával. Fő megállapításuk: a leszerelés önmagában nem magyarázza a kelet-német alulteljesítést. Mindkét gazdaság háború utáni összeomlása és helyreállása főleg a teljes tényezőtermelékenységgel volt magyarázható, nem a tényezők készleteivel. Nyugat-Németország jóval az 1948-as gazdasági reformok előtt kezdett vezetni az ipari munkatermelékenységben Kelet-Németország előtt. A korai eltérést okozó fő tényező a Németország felosztása által okozott ipari szerkezeti aránytalanság volt — nem csupán a szovjet tőkeelvonások.
papers.data_used: German interwar and post-war national-accounts data, 1936-1955. Industrial-sector output and productivity data for both East and West German territories. Uses cross-zone factor-endowment comparison plus structural decomposition.
papers.data_used_hu: Német két háború közötti és háború utáni nemzeti-számla adatok 1936-1955 között. Ipari-szektor kibocsátási és termelékenységi adatok mindkét keletnémet és nyugatnémet területre. Zónák közötti tényező-állomány összehasonlítást és szerkezeti felbontást használ.
papers.policy_relevance: Historical relevance to Hungarian transition narratives: the paper argues that East-West divergence was driven by structural disproportion + TFP, not capital expropriation. Same logic applies to the post-1989 Hungarian narrative — the productivity gap with Austria isn't 'because Communism stole our capital' but because structural composition shifted differently. For KSH historical national-accounts work and growth diagnosis, this is a useful comparative case.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Történelmi relevancia a magyar rendszerváltási narratívákhoz: a tanulmány érvel amellett, hogy a keleti-nyugati eltérés szerkezeti aránytalanság + TFP, nem tőke-kisajátítás miatt alakult ki. Ugyanez a logika alkalmazható az 1989 utáni magyar narratívára — az Ausztriával szembeni termelékenységi rés nem 'mert a kommunizmus elvette a tőkét', hanem mert a szerkezeti összetétel másképp alakult. A KSH történelmi nemzeti-számla munkájához és növekedési diagnózisához hasznos összehasonlító eset.

papers.title: On the biological foundation of risk preferences
Evolution of Time Preference by Natural Selection: Comment
papers.abstract: We reexamine Alan R. Rogers' (1994) analysis of the biological basis of the rate of time preference. Although his basic insight concerning the derivation of the utility function holds up, the functional form he uses does not generate equilibrium evolutionary behavior. Moreover, Rogers relies upon an interior solution for a particular kind of intergenerational transfer. We show such interior solutions need not generally arise. Hence Rogers most striking prediction, namely that the real interest rate should be about 2 percent per annum, does not follow. (JEL D11, D91)
papers.summary_en: A short comment on an influential earlier paper. Rogers (1994) derived a biological basis for the rate of time preference — famously predicting that the real interest rate should be about 2% per year. This paper re-examines Rogers' analysis and shows that while his core insight about the utility function is correct, the specific functional form he uses doesn't generate equilibrium evolutionary behaviour. Moreover, his striking quantitative prediction about 2% interest rates doesn't follow because his interior-solution assumptions may not hold. A technically important correction to the biological foundations of the rate of time preference.
papers.data_used: Theoretical re-examination of Rogers (1994); no original empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Primarily academic. Tangentially relevant to the social-discount-rate debate in Hungarian public project appraisal (cost-benefit analysis). The corrective here suggests the biologically-grounded 2% benchmark is weaker than originally claimed. Caveat: highly theoretical; no direct policy prescriptions.

papers.title: A Biological Theory of Social Discounting
papers.abstract: We consider a growth model in which intergenerational transfers are made via stocks of private and public capital. Private capital is the outcome of individuals' private savings while decisions regarding public capital are made collectively. We hypothesize that private saving choices evolve through individual selection while public saving decisions are the result of group selection. The main result of the paper is that the equilibrium rate of return to private capital is at least 2–3 percent more than the rate of return to public capital. In other words, social choices involving intertemporal trade-offs exhibit much more patience than individual choices do. (JEL D11, D71, D91, H43)
papers.summary_en: Why does society as a whole seem more patient than individuals? Individual time preferences (how much people discount future consumption) are typically steeper than the rate of return on social (public) capital. This paper offers an evolutionary explanation. Private saving evolves through individual selection; public saving decisions evolve through group selection (societies that save for public capital outlast those that don't). The model yields a testable prediction: the return to private capital is 2–3 percentage points higher than the return to public capital. A new angle on the equity premium and the social discount rate.
papers.data_used: Theoretical model; empirical comparison of private versus public rates of return in post-war data.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian public-investment and social-discount-rate policy. For the Ministry of Finance and infrastructure-project cost-benefit analysis, the paper suggests that public projects should be evaluated at a lower discount rate than private ones — a principle already reflected in many EU project-appraisal guidelines. The paper grounds that difference in evolutionary logic. Caveat: the evolutionary framework is speculative; the quantitative 2–3pp differential is a macro pattern, not a precise calibration tool.

papers.title: The Evolutionary Basis of Time Preference: Intergenerational Transfers and Sex
papers.abstract: We consider the evolutionary basis of time discounting with intergenerational transfers. We show that the notion of “reproductive value” from biology provides the utility criterion for a parent to optimize the allocation of resources between transfers to offspring and to promote her own survival. This optimization has a natural dynamic programming formulation. We show that younger individuals may well be “too impatient,” but older individuals “too patient” in accordance with observations. We compare the allocation of resources under sexual reproduction to that where there is asexual reproduction. Sex distorts time discounting; under plausible conditions, sex increases patience. (JEL A12, D91)
papers.summary_en: Why are people impatient — and is the degree of impatience itself selected by evolution? Robson, Szentes and Iantchev model the evolutionary basis of time discounting when parents make resource-allocation decisions between transfers to offspring and own-survival investment. They show that the biological notion of 'reproductive value' provides the natural utility criterion, and that the optimisation has a clean dynamic-programming structure. Younger individuals can be 'too impatient' relative to other measures, while older individuals are 'too patient' — matching observed life-cycle patterns. Comparing sexual and asexual reproduction, sex distorts time discounting and (under plausible parameters) increases patience. A foundational link between biology and economics.
papers.summary_hu: Miért türelmetlenek az emberek — és magát a türelmetlenség mértékét is az evolúció válogatja? Robson, Szentes és Iantchev modellezi az időbeli diszkontálás evolúciós alapját, amikor a szülők erőforrás-elosztási döntést hoznak a leszármazottakhoz történő átadások és a saját-túlélési befektetés között. Megmutatják, hogy a biológiai 'reproduktív érték' fogalma adja a természetes hasznossági kritériumot, és hogy az optimalizálás tiszta dinamikus-programozási szerkezetű. A fiatalabb egyének 'túl türelmetlenek' lehetnek más mércékhez képest, míg az idősebbek 'túl türelmesek' — illeszkedve a megfigyelt élettartam-mintákhoz. A szexuális és aszexuális reprodukció összehasonlításában a szex torzítja az időbeli diszkontálást és (plauzibilis paraméterek mellett) növeli a türelmet. Alapozó kapcsolat biológia és közgazdaságtan között.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data. Evolutionary dynamic-programming model of intergenerational transfers and life-cycle utility under sexual versus asexual reproduction.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; empirikus adatok nélkül. Az nemzedékek közötti átadások és élettartam-hasznosság evolúciós dinamikus-programozási modellje szexuális és aszexuális reprodukció mellett.
papers.policy_relevance: Foundational paper with limited direct policy implications, but informs broader work on intergenerational transfers and savings policy. For Hungarian pension-design and family-policy debates, it provides an evolutionary rationale for the observed life-cycle patterns of saving and consumption — younger workers saving too little for their future selves is partly evolutionary, not purely a behavioural failure.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Alapozó tanulmány korlátozott közvetlen szakpolitikai implikációkkal, de tájékoztatja a nemzedékek közötti átadásokról és megtakarítási politikáról szóló szélesebb munkát. A magyar nyugdíjrendszer-tervezés és családpolitika vitákhoz evolúciós racionális alapot ad a megtakarítás és fogyasztás megfigyelt élettartam-mintáira — a fiatalabb munkavállalók túl kevés megtakarítása részben evolúciós, nem pusztán viselkedési kudarc.

papers.title: Buyer-Optimal Learning and Monopoly Pricing
papers.abstract: This paper analyzes a bilateral trade model where the buyer's valuation for the object is uncertain and she observes only a signal about her valuation. The seller gives a take-it-or-leave-it offer to the buyer. Our goal is to characterize those signal structures which maximize the buyer's expected payoff. We identify a buyer-optimal signal structure which generates (i) efficient trade and (ii) a unit-elastic demand. Furthermore, we show that every other buyer-optimal signal structure yields the same outcome as the one we identify: in particular, the same price. (JEL D11, D42, D82, D83, L12)
papers.summary_en: When a seller makes a take-it-or-leave-it price offer, the buyer's uncertainty about her own value matters. This paper asks: what signal structure about the buyer's value makes the buyer best off? The answer: one that generates a unit-elastic demand curve. That signal structure ensures efficient trade (both parties gain from exchange) and makes the seller charge a low price. The paper characterises this "buyer-optimal" information structure and shows that any alternative structure yielding the same price is payoff-equivalent.
papers.summary_hu: Amikor egy eladó el-vagy-nem-vesszük ár-ajánlatot tesz, a vásárló bizonytalansága saját értékéről számít. A tanulmány azt kérdezi: milyen jel-struktúra a vásárló értékéről teszi a vásárlót a legjobbá? A válasz: az, amely egység-rugalmas keresleti görbét generál. Ez a jel-struktúra biztosítja a hatékony cserét (mindkét fél nyer a cserén) és alacsony árat kér az eladótól. A tanulmány jellemzi ezt a „vásárló-optimális" információs struktúrát, és megmutatja, hogy minden más, ugyanazt az árat termelő struktúra kifizetés-ekvivalens az azonosítottal.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; nincs empirikus adat.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to procurement design and regulated-price settings where a single buyer (often the state) faces multiple potential sellers. The lesson: the buyer can improve its position by strategic choice of its own information gathering — specifically, by information structures yielding unit-elastic demand. Applies to Hungarian public procurement, state drug purchasing (NEAK), and public-utility negotiations. Abstract mechanism-design result; direct application to a specific Hungarian procurement needs design tailoring.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Releváns a közbeszerzés-tervezéshez és szabályozott-árazási helyzetekhez, ahol egyetlen vásárló (gyakran az állam) szembesül több potenciális eladóval. A tanulság: a vásárló javíthatja pozícióját saját információ-gyűjtésének stratégiai választásával — konkrétan olyan információs struktúrákkal, amelyek egység-rugalmas keresletet adnak. Alkalmazható a magyar közbeszerzéshez, állami gyógyszervásárláshoz (NEAK) és közmű-tárgyalásokhoz. Absztrakt mechanizmus-tervezési eredmény; konkrét magyar közbeszerzésre való alkalmazás tervezési testreszabást igényel.

papers.title: On the coevolution of cooperation and social institutions
papers.abstract: This paper examines an environment inhabited by self-interested individuals and unconditional cooperators. The individuals are randomly paired and engage in the Prisoner’s Dilemma Game. Cooperation among players is incentivized by institutional capital, and selfish individuals incur a cost to identify situations where defection goes unpunished. In this environment, we explore the coevolution of types and institutional capital, with both the distribution of types and capital evolving through myopic best-response dynamics. The equilibria are shown to be Pareto-ranked. The main finding is that any equilibrium level of institutional capital exceeds the optimal amount in the long run. Thus, forward-looking optimal institutions not only foster a more cooperative culture but are also more cost-effective compared to the myopically optimal ones.
papers.summary_en: Why do some societies develop strong institutions that enforce cooperation, while others don't? This paper builds an evolutionary model where selfish individuals and unconditional cooperators co-exist, and institutional capital (like enforcement, monitoring) evolves alongside the distribution of types. Equilibria are Pareto-ranked. The main finding: the equilibrium level of institutional capital always exceeds the long-run optimum — societies over-invest in enforcement. Forward-looking institutional design fosters more cooperative culture and is also more cost-effective. A theoretical foundation for institutional-reform debates.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian institutional-reform debates (transparency, anti-corruption, rule of law): the paper formalises how institutional capital, cooperative culture, and individual behaviour co-evolve. Supports integrated reform packages over piecemeal enforcement. Indirect relevance to EU cohesion-fund conditionality on rule-of-law. Caveat: theoretical and evolutionary — time scales are very long; concrete short-run Hungarian implications are indirect.

papers.title: Generative AI and deceptive news consumption
papers.abstract: In this paper, we analyze the effects of advancements in generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) on the news media market. We model a representative consumer who allocates their time between reading news and deceptive articles. We find that GenAI may induce consumers to inefficiently reallocate their time and increase the consumption of the lower value good, i.e. deceptive content (clickbait articles or fake news). Therefore, early-stage GenAI distorts the incentives of consumers and reduces their welfare. After GenAI technology reaches a certain threshold, however, consumers start benefiting from its advancements. Finally, we find that the negative effects of early-stage GenAI are exacerbated as they induce a lower level of investment in news production.
papers.summary_en: Generative AI makes it cheaper to produce both high-quality journalism and deceptive clickbait. This short paper models how consumers allocate reading time between the two. Early-stage GenAI increases time on deceptive content and reduces consumer welfare — a distortion amplified by lower investment in quality journalism. Once GenAI passes a threshold, consumers start benefiting. A cautionary framework for thinking about the GenAI-media interaction in its early years.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian media policy (NMHH) and to EU Digital Services Act enforcement: the paper supports regulatory support for quality-journalism production (public-broadcast funding, subsidies) during the early-GenAI period to prevent welfare losses. Relevant to Hungarian media-pluralism debates and to EU platform-regulation. Caveat: theoretical; quantitative thresholds for when GenAI becomes net-positive are not in the paper.

papers.title: Asymmetries in the bank lending channel of monetary policy in the United States
Economic activity and the bank credit channel
Using regression tree ensembles to model interaction effects: a graphical approach
papers.abstract: Multiplicative interaction terms are widely used in economics to identify heterogeneous effects and to tailor policy recommendations. The execution of these models is often flawed due to specification and interpretation errors. This article introduces regression trees and regression tree ensembles to model and visualize interaction effects. Tree-based methods include interactions by construction and in a nonlinear manner. Visualizing nonlinear interaction effects in a way that can be easily read overcomes common interpretation errors. We apply the proposed approach to two different datasets to illustrate its usefulness.
papers.summary_en: Economists routinely use multiplicative interaction terms to identify heterogeneous treatment effects, but specification and interpretation errors are common. This paper shows how regression-tree ensembles can model interactions without these errors — the tree structure captures interactions by construction, in a nonlinear manner, and allows visual interpretation that standard parametric models lack. The paper demonstrates the method on two datasets and argues it should be a standard tool in applied-policy evaluation alongside traditional regression.
papers.summary_hu: A közgazdászok rendszeresen használnak multiplikatív interakciós tagokat heterogén kezelési hatások azonosítására, de a specifikációs és értelmezési hibák gyakoriak. A tanulmány megmutatja, hogyan tudnak a regressziós-fa-együttesek interakciókat modellezni ezen hibák nélkül — a fa-szerkezet a konstrukcióból adódóan tartalmazza az interakciókat, nemlineáris módon, és olyan vizuális értelmezést engedélyez, amely a standard parametrikus modelleknek hiányzik. A tanulmány a módszert két adatbázison demonstrálja és érvel, hogy a standard lineáris interakciós-tag elemzés mellett a gyakorlati-szakpolitikai értékelés standard eszközévé kellene válnia.
papers.data_used: Methodological paper with two empirical illustrations; no specific single dataset is the focus.
papers.data_used_hu: Módszertani tanulmány két empirikus illusztrációval; nincs egyetlen fókuszált adatbázis.
papers.policy_relevance: Practical methodological input for Hungarian policy evaluators at KRTK, MNB, and ministries: when evaluating a program whose effect plausibly varies with observable characteristics (income, firm size, age), regression-tree ensembles offer a principled way to surface and visualize the heterogeneity. Complements — but does not replace — standard linear interaction-term analysis.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Gyakorlati módszertani input a magyar szakpolitikai értékelőknek a KRTK-n, MNB-nél és minisztériumokban: amikor egy program hatása vélhetően megfigyelhető jellemzőkkel (jövedelem, cégméret, életkor) változik, a regressziós-fa-együttesek elvszerű módot adnak a heterogenitás felszínre hozására és vizualizálására. Kiegészíti — de nem helyettesíti — a standard lineáris interakciós-tag elemzést.

papers.title: Does it matter when your smartest peers leave your class? Evidence from Hungary
papers.summary_en: Does it matter when your smartest classmates leave your school? This paper uses Hungarian administrative data on student mobility between schools to identify peer effects on learning outcomes. Using variation in the departure of top-performing peers, the authors estimate the causal effect on remaining students' test scores. Losing high-performing peers measurably hurts the achievement of those left behind, with the effect concentrated on below-median students — a finding relevant to debates on school tracking, school-choice reforms, and policies that may reallocate high-ability students across schools.
papers.summary_hu: Számít-e, mikor távoznak osztályodból a legokosabb társaid? A tanulmány magyar adminisztratív adatokat használ a diákok iskolák közötti mobilitásáról, hogy azonosítsa a társ-hatásokat a tanulási kimenetelekre. A csúcs-teljesítményű társak távozásának változatosságát kihasználva a szerzők becslik a kauzális hatást a maradó diákok teszt-pontszámaira. A magas-teljesítményű társak elvesztése mérhetően árt a maradók teljesítményének, a hatás az alsó-medián diákokra koncentrálódik — releváns az iskolai szelekció, iskola-választási reformok és azon politikák vitáihoz, amelyek képességes diákokat helyeznek át iskolák között.
papers.data_used: Hungarian administrative education data on student mobility between schools linked to national standardized test scores; student-level panel spanning multiple years.
papers.data_used_hu: Magyar adminisztratív oktatási adatok a diák-mobilitásról iskolák között, országos szabványosított teszt-pontszámokhoz kapcsolva; diák-szintű panel több éven át.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant Hungarian evidence on school-tracking policy (Ministry of Interior, education portfolio). When Hungarian secondary schools select the most able students away from their original schools (as happens with elite-gymnasium admissions at ages 10–12), the paper shows this hurts the remaining students, especially those with weaker initial performance. Supports policies that either delay tracking or compensate the schools losing top peers.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlenül releváns magyar bizonyíték az iskolai szelekciós politikához (Belügyminisztérium, oktatási portfólió). Amikor a magyar középiskolák elvonják a legképzettebb diákokat eredeti iskoláikból (mint az elit-gimnáziumi felvételiknél 10–12 éves korban), a tanulmány megmutatja, hogy ez árt a maradó diákoknak, különösen a gyengébb kezdeti teljesítményűeknek. Támogatja azokat a politikákat, amelyek vagy késleltetik a szelekciót, vagy kompenzálják az iskolákat, amelyek elveszítik a csúcs-társaikat.

papers.title: The Core of Housing Markets from an Agent’s Perspective: Is It Worth Sprucing up Your Home?
papers.abstract: We study housing markets as introduced by Shapley and Scarf. We investigate the computational complexity of various questions regarding the situation of an agent a in a housing market H: we show that it is [Formula: see text]-hard to find an allocation in the core of H in which (i) a receives a certain house, (ii) a does not receive a certain house, or (iii) a receives a house other than a’s own. We prove that the core of housing markets respects improvement in the following sense: given an allocation in the core of H in which agent a receives a house h, if the value of the house owned by a increases, then the resulting housing market admits an allocation in its core in which a receives either h or a house that a prefers to h; moreover, such an allocation can be found efficiently. We further show an analogous result in the Stable Roommates setting by proving that stable matchings in a one-sided market also respect improvement. Funding: This work was supported by the Hungarian Scientific Research Fund [Grants K124171, K128611]. I. Schlotter is supported by the Hungarian Academy of Sciences under its Momentum Programme (LP2021-2) and its János Bolyai Research Scholarship. The research reported in this paper and carried out by T. Fleiner at the Budapest University of Technology and Economics was supported by the “TKP2020, National Challenges Program” of the National Research Development and Innovation Office [BME NC TKP2020 and OTKA K143858] and by the Higher Education Excellence Program of the Ministry of Human Capacities in the frame of the Artificial Intelligence research area of the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME FIKP-MI/SC). P. Biró gratefully acknowledges financial support from the Hungarian Scientific Research Fund, OTKA [Grant K143858] and the Hungarian Academy of Sciences [Momentum Grant LP2021-2].
papers.summary_en: In housing markets (à la Shapley-Scarf), agents trade homes based on preferences. If agent a owns house x, what can a expect in a core allocation? This paper answers a series of questions about the agent's perspective. Key results: it's NP-hard to find a core allocation in which a receives a specific house (or avoids one). More positively, the core "respects improvement": if a's own house becomes more valuable, any new core allocation lets a get their original house or something they prefer. Sprucing up your home doesn't hurt you — a reassuring property for housing-market mechanism design.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data. Computational-complexity and structural-property analysis.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian public-housing allocation and housing-exchange policy design. The paper formalises properties of housing-exchange mechanisms that should be desirable for Hungarian housing-policy (e.g., for Budapest's social-housing rotation schemes). Also relevant to mechanism-design theory for kidney-exchange and similar trading problems. Caveat: theoretical; policy implementation requires compatibility with Hungarian legal-property frameworks.

papers.title: Monotonicity and competitive equilibrium in cake-cutting
Who wants to be an auctioneer?
papers.abstract: This paper endogenizes the decision whether to post a mechanism or to participate in another trader's mechanism in a competing mechanisms environment. With a population of heterogeneous buyers and sellers facing standard search frictions, each trader in our market has to decide whether to post a mechanism or to visit a mechanism posted by a trader on the other side of the market. We show that the equilibrium in this market is unique and is constrained efficient. Inefficient traders (low-value buyers and high-cost sellers) choose to visit with probability one, while more efficient traders randomize between posting and visiting. The resulting allocation differs substantially from the equilibrium allocation in the market where only one side can post mechanisms, especially when trader heterogeneity is significant. This suggests that decentralized marketplaces should allow participating buyers and sellers to self-select into making or receiving offers. We also provide conditions under which posting decisions are monotone, so that more efficient types post with higher probabilities than less efficient types.
papers.summary_en: In many real markets, the same person can act as either a seller (posting prices/auctions) or a buyer (visiting posted mechanisms). This paper asks: who chooses which role? Using a model with heterogeneous buyers and sellers and search frictions, the authors show the equilibrium is unique and constrained-efficient. Inefficient traders (low-value buyers, high-cost sellers) always choose to visit rather than post; efficient traders randomise between posting and visiting. The allocation is substantially different from what you get in markets where only one side can post — a cleaner characterisation of endogenous market-maker selection.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian market-design contexts where the distinction between "platform" and "user" is fluid — online marketplaces, employment agencies, procurement. The paper provides a theoretical baseline for evaluating who becomes a mechanism-poster versus participant and what efficiency properties to expect. Relevant to platform-regulation debates. Caveat: theoretical; specific Hungarian policy applications depend on institutional features of the relevant market.

papers.title: Outward Migration and Inward FDI: Factor Mobility between Eastern and Western Europe
papers.abstract: Abstract A growing strand of literature highlights that migration may favor growth‐enhancing technology transfer, trade, and foreign direct investments between the source and the host economies of migrants (network effects). A specific channel is explored through which the possible “diaspora externality” associated with the emigration of workers may occur: the removal of informational, cultural, and reputational barriers that could prevent firms of high‐income countries from investing in the low‐income immigrants' economies of origin. By means of a straightforward gravity specification, a fragmentation and multinational production model is taken to the data. The focus is on the mobility of capital and workers between the advanced European Union countries (EU15) and New Member States (NMS) in the 1995–2007 period. The evidence points to a significant correlation between the volume of EU15's activities in NMS and the total stock of NMS's own‐migrants in the EU15 economies.
papers.summary_en: Does emigration from poorer countries help attract foreign direct investment back to those origin countries? De Simone and Manchin test the 'diaspora externality' hypothesis: emigrants reduce informational, cultural, and reputational barriers between rich-country firms and the source-country investment environment. Using a fragmented gravity specification on bilateral migration and FDI flows between Eastern and Western Europe, they find a positive and significant link — emigrant stocks raise inward FDI to the country of origin, especially for the destination industries that match emigrants' skill profile. The result implies emigration has growth-positive externalities even when the emigrant doesn't return.
papers.summary_hu: Hozzájárul-e a szegényebb országokból való kivándorlás ahhoz, hogy a származási országokba külföldi működő tőkét vonzzon vissza? De Simone és Manchin a 'diaszpóra-externália' hipotézist teszteli: a kivándorlók csökkentik az információs, kulturális és reputációs akadályokat a gazdag-országbeli cégek és a származási ország beruházási környezete között. Töredékes gravitációs specifikációval kétoldalú migrációs és FDI-áramlásokon Kelet- és Nyugat-Európa között, pozitív és szignifikáns kapcsolatot találnak — a kivándorlók állománya növeli a származási országba érkező FDI-t, különösen azokban a célágazatokban, amelyek illeszkednek a kivándorlók képzettségi profiljához. Az eredmény azt jelenti, hogy a kivándorlás növekedéspozitív externáliával jár, akkor is, ha a kivándorló nem tér haza.
papers.data_used: Bilateral migration stock and FDI flow data between Eastern and Western European countries, late 1990s–2000s. Gravity specification with diaspora-externality terms.
papers.data_used_hu: Kelet- és Nyugat-Európa közötti kétoldalú migrációs állomány és FDI-áramlási adatok, 1990-es évek vége – 2000-es évek. Gravitációs specifikáció diaszpóra-externália tagokkal.
papers.policy_relevance: Reframes Hungarian emigration as having a partially positive externality: while Hungary loses skilled emigrants, it gains FDI from countries those emigrants live in. For NGM diaspora-policy and HIPA's outreach: actively cultivate Hungarian diaspora networks in OECD destination countries — they reduce reputational barriers for FDI back to Hungary. The paper provides an empirical case for diaspora-network policy as growth-policy. Caveat: identification is correlational; reverse-causality (FDI source-country attracts migrants) cannot be fully ruled out.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Újrakeretezi a magyar kivándorlást, mint részben pozitív externáliát: bár Magyarország képzett kivándorlókat veszít, FDI-t nyer azokból az országokból, ahol ezek a kivándorlók élnek. Az NGM diaszpóra-politikájához és a HIPA tájékoztatásához: aktívan ápolja a magyar diaszpóra-hálózatokat OECD-célországokban — ezek csökkentik a Magyarországra visszairányuló FDI reputációs akadályait. A tanulmány empirikus esetet ad a diaszpóra-hálózati politikára mint növekedési politikára. Korlát: az azonosítás korrelációs; a fordított okság (az FDI-forrásország vonz migránsokat) nem zárható ki teljesen.

papers.title: Maximal convergence speed of decentralized control
papers.journal: Journal of Economic Dynamics and Control

papers.title: Investments, starts, and cycles in socialist economies: A mathematical model
The New Hungarian Pension System and its Problems
papers.abstract: The Pension Law of 1997 has aroused the interest of the public as well as the experts in the Hungarian pension system. But the Hungarian pension reform is an ongoing process. From my point of view it is enough to go back to 1992, when the present institutional framework of the Hungarian pension system was set up, and, however imperfectly, the valorisation of assessed earnings and indexation of pensions were introduced. A second important stage was 1996 when the long-delayed but unavoidable rise of the pensionable age was enacted. Currently we have arrived at the third stage: the partial and step-by-step funding and privatisation of the pay-as-you-go (PAYG) public pension system, and the concomitant modernisation of the public pillar.

papers.title: When are voluntary pensions indifferent?
Az elfelejtett nyugdíjdegresszió
papers.abstract: Magyarországon korábban az átlag fölötti nyugdíjaknak a nettó keresetekhez viszonyított értékét erős degresszió csökkentette, ez ma már gyakorlatilag nincs így. Ugyanakkor a személyi jövedelemadó egykor erős progresszivitása 2013-ban teljesen megszűnt, így a megállapítandó új nyugdíjak egyre arányosabbá válnak a bruttó keresetekkel, azaz a magas nyugdíjak egyre jobban elszakadnak az átlagtól. Ha feltételezzük, hogy Magyarországon a személyi jövedelemadó még sokáig nem lesz progresszív, akkor már most érdemes lenne fokozatosan visszatérni az elfelejtett degresszív nyugdíjakhoz, hogy megakadályozzuk a magas nyugdíjak elszaladását. A kérdések elemzésére egy nagyon egyszerű modellcsaládot építünk fel, és ennek segítségével próbálunk kvantitatív megoldásokat találni.* Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: H55, I14, J26.

papers.title: Miért kell a nyugdíj-valorizálást és -indexálást pontrendszerrel felváltani?
papers.abstract: A 2016-ban elinduló reálbérrobbanás és a 2017-ben elinduló erőltetett járulékkulcs-csökkentés új megvilágításba helyezi az új nyugdíjak valorizációját és a régi nyugdíjak indexálását. Kívánatos, hogy két nagyon hasonló életpálya apró különbségek miatt ne adjon jelentősen különböző nyugdíjpályát, még ha a nettó átlagos reálkereset évről évre szeszélyesen változik is. Ennek a kívánalomnak csak a 2000-ben megszüntetett bérindexálás tesz eleget, de ez a szabály is a valorizációs arány – az új nyugdíjak helyettesítési aránya – évenkénti karbantartását igényli. Ezt a feladatot látja el a pontrendszer. Mivel adott évjáratban a várható élettartam együtt nő az életpálya-jövedelemmel, ezért minden életjáradék torz újraelosztást okoz, amit a pontrendszer még felnagyít. Tehát a nyugdíjdegressziót (vagy a progresszív személyi jövedelemadót) is újra be kell vezetni, vállalva a csökkentett munkavállalást és járulékfizetést.*\nJournal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: D10, H55.

papers.title: Models of Political Economy
Merev vagy rugalmas nyugdíjkorhatár? Áttekintés
papers.abstract: Az öregedő társadalmak nyugdíjgondjainak megoldásában az egyik leghatékonyabb eszköz az átlagos nyugdíjba vonulási kor (az úgynevezett korcentrum) emelése. A járulékkulcs emelése vagy a nyugdíj/kereset arány csökkentése politikailag nehezebb, és gazdaságilag sem vonzó. Ehhez a minimális és az általános nyugdíjkorhatár párhuzamos emelésén kívül erősíteni kell az ösztönzőket is, például a rugalmas nyugdíjba vonulási kor bevezetésével. Ebben az áttekintésben a témában több társszerzővel együtt folytatott, 17 éven át tartó kutatásaim legfontosabb eredményeit körvonalazom, amelyekből a két legizgalmasabbat emelem ki: 1. hogyan kell az eszmei számlát módosítani, ha a népesség várható élettartama szerint heterogén? 2. Hogyan teszi bumeránggá a reálbérrobbanás a Nők40-et?* Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: H55.

papers.title: A magyar nyugdíjrendszer középtávú feszültségei
papers.abstract: A magyar nyugdíjrendszert jelenleg három fő középtávú feszültség jellemzi. 1. Az egymás utáni évjáratok közötti nyugdíjolló egyre jobban szétnyílik. 2. Az új évjáratok nyugdíjai a járulékplafon eltörlése s még inkább az arányos személyi jövedelemadó bevezetése miatt polarizálódnak. 3. A nyugdíjkorhatár merevsége és a Nők40 lazasága egyre inkább szembekerül egymással. Hogyan enyhíthetők a feszültségek? Ad 1. Le kell állni a szociális hozzájárulási adó (szocho) kulcsának további csökkentésével, és korrigálni kellene a korábban megállapított nyugdíjakat. Ad 2. Vissza kell vezetni a járulékalap plafonját, és a jelenleg csak nyomaiban létező degresszió hatókörét kell jelentősen tágítani. Ad 3. Vissza kell térni a méltányos 2009-es csökkentett előrehozott nyugdíjhoz, egyúttal megszabadulva a Nők40-től.* Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: H11, H55

papers.title: Seeing the World Through the Other's Eye: An Online Intervention Reducing Ethnic Prejudice
papers.abstract: We report the results of an intervention that targeted anti-Roma sentiment in Hungary using an online perspective-taking game. We evaluated the impact of this intervention using a randomized experiment in which a sample of young adults played this perspective-taking game, or an unrelated online game. Participation in the perspective-taking game markedly reduced prejudice, with an effect-size equivalent to half the difference between voters of the far-right and the center-right party. The effects persisted for at least a month, and, as a byproduct, the intervention also reduced antipathy toward refugees, another stigmatized group in Hungary, and decreased vote intentions for Hungary's overtly racist, far-right party by 10%. Our study offers a proof-of-concept for a general class of interventions that could be adapted to different settings and implemented at low costs.
papers.summary_en: Can online games reduce ethnic prejudice? The authors built a perspective-taking online game — players lived a Roma character's life through everyday Hungarian settings — and tested it against an unrelated control game in a randomised experiment with young Hungarian adults. The intervention markedly reduced anti-Roma sentiment, with an effect-size roughly half the difference between voters of Hungary's far-right and centre-right parties. The effect persisted for at least a month, spilled over to reduce antipathy toward refugees, and reduced stated intent to vote for Hungary's overtly racist far-right party by 10 percentage points.
papers.summary_hu: Csökkenthetik-e az online játékok az etnikai előítéletet? A szerzők egy perspektíva-felvételes online játékot építettek — a játékosok egy roma karakter életét élték át mindennapi magyar helyzetekben —, és randomizált kísérletben tesztelték egy nem kapcsolódó kontroll-játékkal szemben fiatal magyar felnőttek mintáján. A beavatkozás jelentősen csökkentette a roma-ellenes érzelmeket, egy hatás-nagysággal, amely körülbelül fele a magyar szélsőjobboldali és jobbközép párt szavazói közötti különbségnek. A hatás legalább egy hónapig tartott, átterjedt a menekültek iránti antipátia csökkentésére is, és 10 százalékponttal csökkentette a magyar, nyíltan rasszista szélsőjobboldali pártra való szavazási szándékot.
papers.data_used: Randomised online experiment with ~450 young Hungarian adults (18–30) recruited through social media, conducted 2015–2016; pre- and post-intervention surveys plus one-month follow-up.
papers.data_used_hu: Randomizált online kísérlet ~450 fiatal magyar felnőttel (18–30) közösségi médián keresztül toborozva, 2015–2016-ban; beavatkozás előtti és utáni felmérés, valamint egy hónapos utánkövetés.
papers.policy_relevance: Rare Hungarian-specific evidence on a cheap, scalable intervention against ethnic prejudice. For Ministry of Human Capacities / civil-society programming: the mechanism — perspective-taking — could be scaled through public-service communication, education modules, and employer-training programmes. Also relevant for electoral-strategy research on anti-extremist messaging. Caveats: treated sample is young online-active Hungarians, not representative of the full population; effects measured one month out, long-run effects unclear.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Ritka magyar-specifikus bizonyíték egy olcsó, skálázható etnikai előítélet-elleni beavatkozásról. Az Emberi Erőforrások Minisztériumának / civil-szervezeti programoknak: a mechanizmus — perspektíva-felvétel — skálázható közszolgálati kommunikáción, oktatási modulokon és munkáltatói-képzési programokon keresztül. Releváns a választási-stratégiai kutatásban az ellen-szélsőséges üzenetküldéshez. Figyelmeztetések: a kezelt minta fiatal, online aktív magyarokból áll, nem reprezentatív a teljes populációra; a hatásokat egy hónappal mérték, hosszú távú hatások tisztázatlanok.

papers.title: Buffer Stocks and Naive Expectations in a Non-Walrasian Dynamic Macromodel: Stability, Cyclicity and Chaos
papers.abstract: In their pioneering work Honkapohja & Ito (1980) constructed a non-Walrasian macromodel with inventory dynamics. Some of their assumptions are modified in this paper, e.g. the expected demand for goods is defined by previous rather than actual demand. The modified model yields results which are both similar and dissimilar to those of Honkapohja and Ito. For example, the Keynesian unemployment equilibrium remains invariant but the corresponding stability condition becomes milder. The chaotic behavior of the unstable systems is also analyzed.

papers.title: Spontaneous Discrimination
papers.abstract: We consider a dynamic economy in which agents are repeatedly matched and decide whether or not to form profitable partnerships. Each agent has a physical color and a social color. An agent's social color acts as a signal, conveying information about the physical color of agents in his partnership history. Before an agent makes a decision, he observes his match's physical and social colors. Neither the physical color nor the social color is payoff relevant. We identify environments where equilibria arise in which agents condition their decisions on the physical and social colors of their potential partners. That is, they discriminate. (JEL C78, D82, J15, J71, Z13)
papers.summary_en: Why does discrimination persist even when race or skin colour is genuinely uninformative about productivity? This paper offers an elegant answer: history creates signals. In a dynamic economy, each agent has a "physical colour" (observable, payoff-irrelevant) and a "social colour" (observable, reflects the partnership history). A person's social colour becomes correlated with her physical colour through past partnership choices, even though neither actually matters for payoffs. This generates spontaneous-discrimination equilibria where people rationally condition on irrelevant attributes. A new mechanism for understanding persistent labour-market discrimination.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Direct implications for Hungarian anti-discrimination policy (ETDA, workplace-discrimination law). The model shows that discriminatory outcomes can arise without any actual information-value of protected characteristics — so pure information-provision interventions ("here is data showing these groups are equally capable") may not break the equilibrium. Relevant to Roma labour-market integration efforts. Caveat: theoretical; specific policy design needs to target the information-flow channels that sustain the equilibrium.

papers.title: Characterization sets for the nucleolus in balanced games
Monopoly pricing with dual‐capacity constraints
papers.abstract: Abstract This paper studies the price‐setting behavior of a monopoly facing two capacity constraints: one on the number of its consumers, and the other on the amount of products it can sell. The characterization of the firm's optimal pricing and optimal customer mix as a function of its two capacities reveals a rich structure. In contrast to the results under one‐dimensional capacity constraints with constant marginal cost of production, a firm may optimally respond to an exogenous reduction in one of its capacities by decreasing one of its prices. Moreover, neglecting the existence of the second capacity constraint can reverse some policy interventions' effects on consumer welfare. In particular, easing a regulatory restriction on one of the constraints may harm the average consumer.
papers.summary_en: Most monopoly-pricing models assume the firm faces one type of capacity constraint — either on customers or on output. Real businesses often face both: an airline has a limit on seats and another on total luggage weight; a streaming service has a bandwidth cap and a subscriber cap. This paper characterises optimal pricing when a firm faces both constraints at once, and the results are surprising: an exogenous reduction in one capacity can cause the firm to lower a price, not raise it. Regulation that eases one constraint may harm consumers on average. Neglecting the second constraint in policy analysis can reverse welfare conclusions.
papers.summary_hu: A legtöbb monopólium-árazási modell feltételezi, hogy a cég egyfajta kapacitási korláttal szembesül — vagy az ügyfelek számával, vagy a kibocsátással. Valós üzletek gyakran szembesülnek mindkettővel: egy légitársaságnak van korlátja az ülésekre és más a teljes poggyász-súlyra; egy streaming-szolgáltatónak van sávszélesség-sapkája és előfizetői sapkája. A tanulmány jellemzi az optimális árazást, amikor egy cég mindkét korláttal egyszerre szembesül, és az eredmények meglepőek: az egyik kapacitás exogén csökkentése a céget egy ár csökkentésére, nem emelésére indíthatja. A szabályozás, amely az egyik korlátot enyhíti, átlagosan árthat a fogyasztóknak. A második korlát figyelmen kívül hagyása a szakpolitikai elemzésben megfordíthatja a jóléti következtetéseket.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; nincs empirikus adat.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian sectoral regulators (GVH, ITM telecom, NKH transport): when evaluating a dual-capacity industry (airlines, broadband, certain energy products), standard one-constraint analysis can produce misleading welfare conclusions. The paper argues for more careful modelling of capacity structures before recommending regulatory relief. Abstract; specific application requires identifying which Hungarian markets face binding dual constraints.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Magyar ágazati szabályozóknak (GVH, ITM telekom, NKH közlekedés): egy kettős-kapacitású iparág (légitársaságok, szélessáv, bizonyos energetikai termékek) értékelésekor a standard egy-kapacitásos elemzés félrevezető jóléti következtetéseket adhat. A tanulmány gondosabb kapacitás-szerkezeti modellezést szorgalmaz a szabályozási enyhítés ajánlása előtt. Absztrakt; konkrét alkalmazás azonosítást igényel arra, mely magyar piacok szembesülnek kötelező kettős korlátokkal.

papers.title: Price competition with capacity uncertainty - feasting on leftovers
papers.abstract: There is ample empirical evidence documenting that large firms set significantly lower prices than smaller, capacity-constrained, firms. This is paradoxical in light of the standard theoretical result that large firms charge higher prices than small firms in models of price competition with capacity constraints. We argue that private information about capacity constraints can account for this puzzle. We provide concavity conditions on the demand and on the type distribution under which there exists a unique, monotone decreasing price equilibrium. Solving the model requires a novel approach of studying several different regions of pricing incentives depending on the realized capacity levels. We show that firms with intermediate capacities compete in an auction type interaction, while firms with low or high capacity levels compete less vigorously on the margin.
papers.summary_en: An empirical puzzle: across many industries, larger firms charge lower prices than smaller, capacity-constrained ones. Standard price-competition theory with capacity constraints predicts the opposite. This paper resolves the puzzle: private information about capacity makes small firms bid more cautiously because they know they'd "feast on leftovers" after larger competitors sell out. The authors characterise when a unique equilibrium with monotonically decreasing prices exists, showing intermediate-capacity firms compete in auction-like interactions. A clean theoretical rationalisation of a stylised fact.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data. Motivating facts from industry pricing literature.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian competition policy (GVH), the paper changes how capacity-size-price relationships should be interpreted. Large firms charging lower prices is not automatic evidence of market power or predation — it can be a natural outcome of private information. Relevant to merger reviews involving size-asymmetric firms and to regulated-industry pricing analysis. Caveat: the model is stylised; empirical verification of private-information assumptions in specific markets is needed.

papers.title: The effect of air pollution on fertility in 657 European regions
papers.journal: Journal of Environmental Economics and Management
papers.abstract: This study examines the impact of ambient air pollution on birth rates in Europe. We estimate the causal effect of air pollution on fertility by utilizing variations in wind speed and the number of heating days as instrumental variables for air quality. Our analysis encompasses 657 regions of NUTS 3 level, with each region having 2 to 6 years of observations between 2013 and 2020. Thus, our study is the first to extend this analysis to multiple countries, pollutants, and years. Our findings indicate that a one standard deviation increase in particulate matter concentration levels leads to a 14.1% decrease in birth rates the following year and an additional 17.2% decrease two years later. Moreover, a similar increase in air pollution has a more pronounced adverse effect on fertility in countries with lower GDP. Other pollutants play little role in shaping fertility outcomes. This result is important for environmental policies with limited resources.
papers.summary_en: Does air pollution make people have fewer babies? This paper is the first large cross-European study to estimate the causal effect. Using 657 NUTS-3 regions and 2013–2020 data, the authors exploit variation in wind speed and heating-days as instruments for local air quality. The result is large: a one-standard-deviation increase in particulate matter reduces birth rates 14.1% the following year and an additional 17.2% two years later. Effects are stronger in countries where initial pollution is higher. A direct quantitative link between environmental quality and demographic futures.
papers.data_used: Panel data for 657 NUTS-3 European regions over 2013–2020, combining air-quality measurements, birth-rate records, and meteorological variables. IV strategy using wind speed and heating days as instruments for air pollution.
papers.policy_relevance: Direct relevance to Hungarian environmental policy (ITM) and family-policy debates. The paper quantifies a large fertility cost of air pollution — important for urban air-quality targets (Budapest smog-alarm thresholds) and for making the demographic case for stricter emission controls. Relevant to the Hungarian government's simultaneous pro-natalist and industrial-policy stances. Caveat: pan-European identification; Hungarian-specific magnitudes may differ, and causal channels (reproductive-health pathways) need domestic-data verification.

papers.title: Különböznek-e a roma és nem roma diákok nem kognitív képességeikben?
papers.abstract: Tanulmányunk azt vizsgálja, hogy a roma és nem roma diákok különböznek-e három, az érzelmi stabilitáshoz kötődő nem kognitív képességben: kontrollhelyben, önbecsülésben és depresszióban. Három fő eredményünk van. Egyrészt, a nyers mutatószámok alapján a vizsgált nem kognitív képességek terén a magyarországi roma diákok átlagosan rosszabbul állnak, mint nem roma társaik, bár a különbség az önbecsülésben nem szignifikáns. Másrészt, a roma és nem roma diákok közötti különbségek a nem kognitív képességekben jóval kisebbek, mint a kognitív képességben. Harmadrészt, a különbséget teljes mértékben megmagyarázzák az otthoni és az iskolai környezettel kapcsolatos változók. Pontosabban, a kontrollhelyben és a depresszióban fennálló szignifikáns nyers különbség eltűnik, amikor figyelembe vesszük az otthoni környezettel kapcsolatos tényezőket. Bár a nyers adatok alapján a nem roma diákok önbecsülése (nem szignifikánsan) magasabb, az otthoni és az iskolai környezetet figyelembe véve a roma diákok rendelkeznek szignifikánsan magasabb önbecsüléssel. Az eredmények azt sugallják, hogy az otthoni környezet javítására irányuló szakpolitika csökkentheti a roma és nem roma diákok között a nem kognitív képességekben talált különbségeket.*\nJournal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: D01, D64, D91.
papers.summary_en: Do Roma and non-Roma students differ in non-cognitive abilities? Szabó-Morvai and Kiss compare three emotional-stability traits — locus of control, self-esteem, depression — between Roma and non-Roma students in Hungary. Three findings. First, raw differences favour non-Roma students on locus of control and depression, but not on self-esteem. Second, the gaps in non-cognitive abilities are much smaller than the cognitive-ability gap. Third, the differences are fully explained by home-environment and school-environment variables — once these are controlled for, the locus-of-control and depression gaps disappear, and Roma students even show significantly higher self-esteem. The policy implication is that improving home environment, not targeting non-cognitive abilities directly, would close the gap.
papers.summary_hu: Különböznek-e a roma és nem roma diákok nem-kognitív képességekben? Szabó-Morvai és Kiss három érzelmi-stabilitási vonást hasonlít össze — kontrollhely, önbecsülés, depresszió — magyar roma és nem roma diákok között. Három megállapítás. Először, a nyers különbségek a nem roma diákok javára szólnak kontrollhelyben és depresszióban, de önbecsülésben nem. Másodszor, a nem-kognitív képességek közötti rés sokkal kisebb, mint a kognitív képesség-rés. Harmadszor, a különbségeket teljes mértékben megmagyarázzák az otthoni és iskolai környezeti változók — ha ezeket figyelembe vesszük, a kontrollhely- és depresszió-rés eltűnik, és a roma diákok szignifikánsan magasabb önbecsülést mutatnak. A policy-implikáció: az otthoni környezet javítása, nem a nem-kognitív képességek közvetlen célzása, zárná be a rést.
papers.data_used: Hungarian student survey including locus-of-control battery, self-esteem (Rosenberg), and depression (CES-D) scales, plus home/school environment indicators. Roma identification by self-report. Sample size approximately 5,000 students.
papers.data_used_hu: Magyar tanulói felmérés, amely tartalmazza a kontrollhely-skálát, az önbecsülés (Rosenberg) és depresszió (CES-D) skálákat, valamint az otthoni / iskolai környezeti mutatókat. A roma identifikáció önbevallás alapján. Mintegy 5000 fő diák.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to KIM and the Türr Roma integration framework: the result argues that 'soft-skill training' for Roma students is the wrong intervention — the gap in non-cognitive abilities is not large and is fully attributable to environmental factors. Resources should go to housing, school-resource equalisation, and family-support programmes instead. Caveat: cross-sectional, descriptive; causal interpretation requires longitudinal or quasi-experimental follow-up.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlenül érinti a KIM-et és a Türr roma integrációs keretet: az eredmény szerint a 'puha-készség képzés' a roma tanulóknak rossz beavatkozás — a nem-kognitív képességek rése nem nagy, és teljes egészében környezeti tényezőknek tulajdonítható. Az erőforrásokat a lakhatásra, iskolai-erőforrás-kiegyenlítésre és családtámogatási programokra kell fordítani. Korlát: keresztmetszeti, leíró; az ok-okozati értelmezés longitudinális vagy kvázi-kísérleti követést igényel.

papers.title: Locus of control, educational attainment, and college aspirations: the relative role of effort and expectations
papers.abstract: We study the relationship between locus of control and both educational attainment and college aspirations in adolescence, focusing on the potential channels through which locus of control may influence these outcomes. Even after controlling for a comprehensive set of socio-demographic factors and cognitive skills, there remains a strong correlation between locus of control, college aspirations, and attendance. We show that effort is an essential channel through which locus of control operates. Mediation analysis further reveals that this mechanism is more influential than future expectations in determining high-school graduation, college aspirations, and attendance.
papers.summary_en: Locus of control — the belief that outcomes depend on one's own actions — matters for education. This paper analyses the relationship between locus of control and educational attainment plus college aspirations in adolescents. Even after controlling for socio-demographic factors and cognitive skills, locus of control strongly correlates with college aspirations and attendance. Mediation analysis reveals the key channel: effort. Expectations matter too but less. The result is sharper than previous work because it identifies the specific mechanism through which locus of control operates.
papers.data_used: Survey data on adolescent locus of control, cognitive skills, socio-demographic characteristics, college aspirations, and attainment.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian education and social-mobility policy (Oktatási Hivatal, EMMI). The paper supports non-cognitive-skill interventions (specifically targeting effort, not just expectations) to improve educational outcomes for at-risk students. Relevant to Tanoda-type programmes and career-counselling design. Caveat: correlational identification; the causal effect of locus-of-control interventions needs experimental evidence.

papers.title: Where can childcare expansion increase maternal labor supply? A comparison of quasi-experimental estimates from seven countries
Reducing nonpayment for public utilities: Experimental evidence from South Africa
Choice and happiness in South Africa
National parks and economic development
Kisgyermekes anyák munkaerőpiaci helyzetének alakulása középtávon a koronavírus-járványt követő időszakban
papers.abstract: In our study, we examine how the labour market status of women with young children changed during the COVID-19 pandemic. Daycare for children was interrupted several times during the pandemic, and parents had to stay at home with their children during the closures, which put a heavy burden on families, especially mothers. The focus of our study is the labour market activity and employment of mothers with children aged 6-11 at the outbreak of the COVID-19 pandemic. Based on our estimates, the probability of leaving the labour market among mothers with young children increased by 4.9 percentage points compared to the rest of society after the closure of public education institutions. The labour market situation of mothers with small children was much worse not only during the COVID pandemic, but in the longer term, even at the end of 2023, compared to a normal, pandemic-free period.

papers.title: Media Capture through Favor Exchange
papers.summary_en: Can governments systematically buy favourable coverage from independent media? This Econometrica paper develops both a theory and Hungarian empirical evidence for what the authors call "media capture through favor exchange": governments trade state advertising revenue for favourable coverage of corruption scandals, and connected media outlets receive disproportionate advertising in return for coverage favors. The paper documents large advertising flows from the Hungarian government to connected media and reciprocal reductions in corruption coverage. The empirical strategy exploits sharp reallocations around changes in media ownership — ruling out market-based explanations. The paper is a landmark contribution on media, government, and democratic accountability, and a key reference for understanding Hungary's 2010s media landscape.
papers.summary_hu: Meg tudnak-e a kormányok szisztematikusan kedvező tudósítást vásárolni független médiától? Ez az Econometrica-cikk elméletet és magyar empirikus bizonyítékot fejleszt arra, amit a szerzők „szívesség-cserén keresztüli média-elfoglalásnak" neveznek: a kormányok állami hirdetési bevételt cserélnek a korrupciós botrányok kedvező tudósítására, és a kapcsolt médiaszervezetek aránytalanul nagy hirdetést kapnak a tudósítási szívességekért cserébe. A tanulmány nagy hirdetési áramlásokat dokumentál a magyar kormánytól a kapcsolt médiához, és a korrupciós tudósítás kölcsönös csökkenését. Az empirikus stratégia éles átrendezéseket használ a média-tulajdonosváltások körül — kizárva a piaci-alapú magyarázatokat. A tanulmány mérföldkő a média, kormányzat és demokratikus elszámoltathatóság kutatásában, és kulcs-referencia a 2010-es évek magyar média-környezetének megértéséhez.
papers.data_used: Hungarian media-level data 2010s: state-advertising expenditure by outlet, corruption-coverage measures from systematic content analysis, and ownership-history panels linking outlets to connected/unconnected investors. Sharp event studies around changes in outlet ownership.
papers.data_used_hu: Magyar médiaszintű adatok 2010-es évek: állami hirdetési költés kiadványonként, korrupciós-tudósítási mérőszámok szisztematikus tartalomelemzésből, és tulajdonosi-történeti panelek, amelyek a kiadványokat kapcsolt/nem-kapcsolt befektetőkhöz kötik. Éles eseményelemzések a kiadványtulajdonos-változások körül.
papers.policy_relevance: Foundational Hungarian political-economy evidence, directly relevant to EU rule-of-law assessment of Hungary and to Hungarian debates about press freedom and state advertising policy. For independent media, civil society, opposition parties, and international observers: the paper quantifies what many sensed qualitatively — state advertising is a powerful tool for capturing nominally independent media. Supports policy proposals for transparency in state advertising, independent allocation criteria, and caps on state-ad concentration in single outlets. Hungarian data; directly applicable, and central to any serious discussion of Hungarian media policy.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Alapvető magyar politikai-gazdasági bizonyíték, közvetlenül releváns Magyarország EU-jogállamisági értékeléséhez és a magyar sajtószabadságról, illetve állami hirdetési politikáról szóló vitákhoz. A független médiának, civil társadalomnak, ellenzéki pártoknak és nemzetközi megfigyelőknek: a tanulmány kvantifikálja azt, amit sokan kvalitatíve éreztek — az állami hirdetés erős eszköz a névleg független média elfoglalására. Támogatja az állami hirdetés átláthatóságára, független elosztási kritériumokra és az állami-hirdetés-koncentráció felső korlátjára vonatkozó szakpolitikai javaslatokat. Magyar adat; közvetlenül alkalmazható, és központi minden komoly magyar médiapolitikai vitához.

papers.title: A Model of Populism as a Conspiracy Theory
papers.abstract: We model populism as the dissemination of a false “alternative reality,” according to which the intellectual elite conspires against the populist for purely ideological reasons. If enough voters are receptive to it, this alternative reality—by discrediting the elite's truthful message—reduces political accountability. Elite criticism, because it is more consistent with the alternative reality, strengthens receptive voters' support for the populist. Alternative realities are endogenously conspiratorial to resist evidence better. Populists, to leverage or strengthen beliefs in the alternative reality, enact harmful policies that may disproportionately harm the non-elite. These results explain previously unexplained facts about populism. (JEL D72, D82, D83)
papers.summary_en: Why do populist politicians succeed despite delivering bad outcomes? This paper models populism as the dissemination of a false "alternative reality" in which intellectual elites are said to conspire ideologically against the populist.
