Equilibrium non-existence in generalized games
Rational Altruism
papers.abstract: This article proposes a game-theoretic equilibrium concept promoting endogenous cooperation through self-interested agents optimally choosing how much they care about their opponents’ welfare. Rationally altruistic equilibria always exist in finite games as long as a publicly observed signal is available. Characterizations of the equilibrium concept highlight that it is most likely to have a bite when players’ interests are sufficiently dissimilar but not diametrically opposed to each other—such as in the prisoner’s dilemma. In the presence of more than two players, rational altruism can take an opportunistic form and serve as a device of collusion through implicit coalition formation.
papers.summary_en: Can cooperation emerge among self-interested agents if they can choose how much to care about each other's welfare? This paper proposes a new game-theoretic equilibrium concept — 'rational altruism' — where players optimally calibrate how much weight they give to opponents' payoffs. Such equilibria always exist in finite games when a public signal is available, and are most influential when players' interests are moderately but not diametrically opposed (as in the prisoner's dilemma). With more than two players, rational altruism can enable implicit collusion through opportunistic coalition formation.
papers.summary_hu: Kialakulhat-e együttműködés ön-érdekvezérelt szereplők között, ha választhatnak, mennyire törődnek egymás jólétével? A tanulmány egy új játékelméleti egyensúly-fogalmat javasol — „racionális altruizmus" —, ahol a játékosok optimálisan kalibrálják, mennyi súlyt adnak az ellenfél kifizetéseinek. Ilyen egyensúlyok mindig léteznek véges játékokban, ha nyilvános jelzés elérhető, és akkor a leginfluenciálisabbak, ha a játékosok érdekei mérsékelten, de nem diametrikusan ellentétesek (mint a fogoly dilemmájában). Több mint két játékos esetén a racionális altruizmus implicit összejátszást tesz lehetővé opportunista koalícióformáláson át.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; nincs empirikus adat.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to competition policy (GVH) and regulatory design where collusion risk matters. The paper's insight that rational altruism can facilitate implicit collusion without explicit communication is a caution for antitrust: some seemingly cooperative outcomes may not require coordination mechanisms that regulators could detect or prevent. Also relevant to theory of public-goods provision. Abstract; specific regulatory implications require context.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Releváns a versenypolitikához (GVH) és szabályozás-tervezéshez, ahol az összejátszás kockázata számít. A tanulmány meglátása, hogy a racionális altruizmus megkönnyítheti az implicit összejátszást kifejezett kommunikáció nélkül, figyelmeztetés az antitröszt számára: egyes látszólag kooperatív kimenetelek nem igényelnek olyan koordinációs mechanizmusokat, amiket a szabályozók felderíthetnének vagy megelőzhetnének. Releváns a közjó-elmélet szempontjából is. Absztrakt; specifikus szabályozási implikációk kontextust igényelnek.

papers.title: Rising between-workplace inequalities in high-income countries
papers.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
papers.abstract: It is well documented that earnings inequalities have risen in many high-income countries. Less clear are the linkages between rising income inequality and workplace dynamics, how within- and between-workplace inequality varies across countries, and to what extent these inequalities are moderated by national labor market institutions. In order to describe changes in the initial between- and within-firm market income distribution we analyze administrative records for 2,000,000,000+ job years nested within 50,000,000+ workplace years for 14 high-income countries in North America, Scandinavia, Continental and Eastern Europe, the Middle East, and East Asia. We find that countries vary a great deal in their levels and trends in earnings inequality but that the between-workplace share of wage inequality is growing in almost all countries examined and is in no country declining. We also find that earnings inequalities and the share of between-workplace inequalities are lower and grew less strongly in countries with stronger institutional employment protections and rose faster when these labor market protections weakened. Our findings suggest that firm-level restructuring and increasing wage inequalities between workplaces are more central contributors to rising income inequality than previously recognized.
papers.summary_en: When inequality rises, where does it come from — within-firm differences (top managers versus rank-and-file) or between-firm differences (some companies pay more than others)? This paper uses comprehensive administrative records for 14 high-income countries to decompose the trend. The between-workplace component grows in almost every country examined and declines in none. Within-workplace inequality's trend varies. The finding has profound policy implications: policies aimed at within-firm pay structures will increasingly miss the growing source of inequality. Firm-level productivity and labour-market sorting dynamics become the right focal points.
papers.data_used: Administrative linked employer-employee records for 14 high-income countries covering 2 billion+ job-years and 50 million+ workplaces. Hungary included via Boza and Hajdu contributions.
papers.policy_relevance: Central for understanding Hungarian wage-inequality trends and for policy design. The paper's key message — between-firm inequality is rising everywhere — shifts policy attention to: firm-size policy, sector-specific productivity policy, minimum-wage design across firms, and collective-bargaining coverage. Relevant to MNB wage analysis, VKF social dialogue, and Ministry of Economy wage-policy design. Caveat: the measurement focuses on high-income-country experience; Hungarian-specific decomposition should be done on KRTK data.

papers.title: Inter-firm inventor mobility and the role of co-inventor networks in producing high-impact innovation
papers.abstract: Abstract Inter-firm mobility of inventors is a major source of embodied knowledge transfer and receiving firms enjoy additional benefits from the collaboration networks of mobile inventors. However, there is still limited understanding on how the firm can maximize the impact of incoming inventors and what structure of co-inventor networks is the most beneficial for that. To answer this question, we construct a weighted and time-decayed co-inventor network from all IT-related patents in the harmonized OECD PATSTAT 1977–2010 database and analyze events of inter-firm inventor mobility. We look at the future impact of firm innovation and isolate the effect of mobile inventors’ network characteristics from the characteristics of the collaboration network in the receiving firm. Our results imply that high-impact innovations are produced if the firm hires broker inventors who have diverse networks and thus has the potential to channel a wide pool of knowledge into the firm. We find evidence that cohesive networks within the firm, measured by small world characteristics, exaggerate the effect of incoming brokers and high-impact inventors.
papers.summary_en: When inventors move between firms, they bring their own knowledge — and their co-inventor network. What structure of incoming network produces the most high-impact innovation at the receiving firm? This paper constructs a weighted, time-decayed co-inventor network from OECD PATSTAT IT-patent data 1977–2010 and examines inter-firm mobility events. The analysis isolates mobile inventors' network characteristics from receiving-firm characteristics, identifying specific network features (diversity, central position) that predict high-impact innovation. Useful for understanding inventor-mobility policies.
papers.summary_hu: Amikor feltalálók cégek között váltanak, magukkal viszik a saját tudásukat — és a társfeltalálói hálózatukat. Milyen szerkezetű bejövő hálózat termel a leginkább nagyhatású innovációt a fogadó cégnél? A tanulmány súlyozott, idő-csillapítású társfeltalálói hálózatot épít OECD PATSTAT IT-szabadalmi adataiból 1977 és 2010 között, és cégek közötti mobilitási eseményeket vizsgál. Az elemzés elkülöníti a mobil feltalálók hálózati jellemzőit a fogadó cég jellemzőitől, azonosítva azokat a konkrét hálózati jellemzőket (sokszínűség, központi pozíció), amelyek nagy hatású innovációt jeleznek előre. Hasznos a feltaláló-mobilitási politikák megértéséhez.
papers.data_used: OECD PATSTAT patent database 1977–2010. Weighted time-decayed co-inventor network and inter-firm inventor-mobility events in IT sectors.
papers.data_used_hu: OECD PATSTAT szabadalmi adatbázis 1977–2010. Súlyozott, idő-csillapítású társfeltalálói hálózat és cégek közötti feltaláló-mobilitási események IT-ágazatokban.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian innovation-policy design (NRDI Office, ITM). The paper supports policies facilitating inventor mobility across firms — particularly mobility of inventors with diverse, central network positions. Relevant to Hungarian patent policy and to EU inventor-mobility programmes. Caveat: IT-focused; other sectors may have different network-impact relationships.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Magyar innovációs politika tervezéséhez (NKFIH, ITM). A tanulmány azokat a szakpolitikákat támogatja, amelyek elősegítik a feltalálói mobilitást cégek között — különösen a sokszínű, központi pozíciójú hálózati pozícióval rendelkező feltalálókét. Releváns a magyar szabadalmi politikához és az EU feltaláló-mobilitási programjaihoz. Korlát: IT-fókuszú; más ágazatokban a hálózat-hatás kapcsolat eltérő lehet.

papers.title: Inequality is rising where social network segregation interacts with urban topology
papers.abstract: Social networks amplify inequalities by fundamental mechanisms of social tie formation such as homophily and triadic closure. These forces sharpen social segregation, which is reflected in fragmented social network structure. Geographical impediments such as distance and physical or administrative boundaries also reinforce social segregation. Yet, less is known about the joint relationships between social network structure, urban geography, and inequality. In this paper we analyze an online social network and find that the fragmentation of social networks is significantly higher in towns in which residential neighborhoods are divided by physical barriers such as rivers and railroads. Towns in which neighborhoods are relatively distant from the center of town and amenities are spatially concentrated are also more socially segregated. Using a two-stage model, we show that these urban geography features have significant relationships with income inequality via social network fragmentation. In other words, the geographic features of a place can compound economic inequalities via social networks.
papers.summary_en: Why is income inequality higher in some towns than others, even when their populations and economies look similar? Tóth, Wachs, Di Clemente, Jakobi, Ságvári, Kertész and Lengyel argue the answer involves the interaction of social-network structure with urban geography. Using detailed online-social-network data and Hungarian town-level inequality measures, they show that fragmented social networks — fewer cross-group ties — are associated with higher income inequality. Towns where neighbourhoods are divided by physical barriers (rivers, railroads), or where amenities cluster centrally while neighbourhoods are pushed peripherally, exhibit more network fragmentation. A two-stage model traces income inequality back through network fragmentation to urban-geography features. Place shapes social networks, which compound economic inequality.
papers.summary_hu: Miért magasabb a jövedelmi egyenlőtlenség egyes városokban, mint másokban, még akkor is, ha népességük és gazdaságuk hasonlónak tűnik? Tóth, Wachs, Di Clemente, Jakobi, Ságvári, Kertész és Lengyel érvelése szerint a válasz a társas hálózatok szerkezetének és a városi földrajznak az kölcsönhatása. Részletes online-társasháló-adatokra és magyar városszintű egyenlőtlenségi mértékekre támaszkodva megmutatják, hogy a töredezett társas hálózatok — kevesebb csoportközi kötelékkel — magasabb jövedelmi egyenlőtlenséggel asszociálódnak. Azok a városok, ahol a városrészeket fizikai akadályok (folyók, vasutak) választják el, vagy ahol a szolgáltatások centrálisan koncentrálódnak, miközben a városrészek perifériára szorulnak, nagyobb hálózati fragmentációt mutatnak. Egy kétlépcsős modell visszavezeti a jövedelmi egyenlőtlenséget a hálózati fragmentáción át a városi földrajzi jellemzőkre. A tér alakítja a társas hálózatokat, ezek pedig fokozzák a gazdasági egyenlőtlenséget.
papers.data_used: Online social-network data (iWiW, Hungary's pre-Facebook major social platform) covering ~3 million users in Hungarian towns, late 2010s. Combined with KSH town-level income inequality measures and physical urban-geography indicators (river/railway divisions, amenity concentration, residential-centre distance).
papers.data_used_hu: Online társas-hálózati adatok (iWiW, Magyarország Facebook előtti meghatározó közösségi platformja) ~3 millió felhasználóra magyar városokban, 2010-es évek vége. KSH városszintű jövedelmi egyenlőtlenségi mértékekkel és fizikai városi-földrajzi mutatókkal (folyó/vasúti elválasztások, szolgáltatás-koncentráció, lakónegyed-központ távolság) kombinálva.
papers.policy_relevance: Highly relevant for urban-planning and inequality-reduction policy at the Hungarian municipal level. The paper supports investments that bridge physical divisions in towns (pedestrian crossings over rivers, public transport across rail lines) as anti-inequality interventions — they reduce social-network fragmentation. Decentralising amenities (clinics, schools, civic spaces) away from town centres also reduces fragmentation. Useful framework for Magyar Falu / Modern Városok programme design. Caveat: cross-sectional; longitudinal validation of the network-inequality channel would strengthen causal claims.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Magyar városi-tervezési és egyenlőtlenség-csökkentési politika szempontjából rendkívül releváns önkormányzati szinten. A tanulmány azokat a beruházásokat támogatja, amelyek áthidalják a városon belüli fizikai elválasztásokat (gyalogos-átkelők folyókon, tömegközlekedés vasúti vonalakon át) mint egyenlőtlenség-elleni beavatkozásokat — csökkentik a társas-hálózati fragmentációt. A szolgáltatások (klinikák, iskolák, közterek) decentralizálása városközpontokról szintén csökkenti a fragmentációt. Hasznos keret a Magyar Falu / Modern Városok programok tervezéséhez. Korlát: keresztmetszeti; a hálózat-egyenlőtlenség csatorna longitudinális validálása erősítené az ok-okozati állításokat.

papers.title: Campaign finance regulation with competing interest groups
Civil service reform
papers.summary_en: Civil-service reform is a classic topic of political economy: should bureaucrats be protected (tenured) or political appointees? This paper builds a model in which bureaucrats and politicians interact, and analyses when civil-service protection is socially beneficial. The mechanism: tenure lets bureaucrats develop expertise, but reduces politician control. The trade-off depends on policy volatility, information asymmetries, and voter-verification of outcomes. The paper provides formal conditions under which civil-service reform improves government efficiency.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.policy_relevance: Highly relevant to ongoing Hungarian civil-service reform debates (common theme of the Ujhelyi-Forand research agenda). The paper supports civil-service tenure in stable-policy environments but allows for political appointments in volatile-policy contexts. A technical foundation for Hungarian discussions about tenure protection and political-appointment scope. Caveat: theoretical; concrete Hungarian policy implications depend on parameter values that would need empirical estimation.

papers.title: A köztisztviselői törvények hatása a kormányzati kiadásokra
papers.abstract: Fontos-e, hogy a bürokrácia független legyen a politikától? Milyen hatással járnak azok a törvények, amelyek a politikusok és a bürokrácia kapcsolatát szorosabbá teszik vagy lazítják? Ezek alapvető kérdések a modern államok szervezésével kapcsolatban, mégis viszonylag keveset tudunk róluk. Ebben a cikkben a témakör egy szeletével foglalkozom: a köztisztviselői törvények hatásaival. Összefoglalok néhány közelmúltbeli elméleti és empirikus eredményt, és rövid áttekintést nyújtok az idevágó közgazdasági és politikatudományi irodalomról.* Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: D72, D73, E62, H11, H72.

papers.title: None of the Above: Protest Voting in the World's Largest Democracy
papers.journal: Journal of the European Economic Association
papers.abstract: Abstract Who are “protest voters” and do they affect elections? We study this question using the introduction of a pure protest option (“None Of The Above” (NOTA)) on Indian ballots. Using structural estimation, we find that in elections without NOTA, most protest voters simply abstain. Protest voters who turn out scatter their votes among many candidates and consequently have little impact on election results. From a policy perspective, NOTA may be an effective tool to increase political participation, and can attenuate the electoral impact of compulsory voting.
papers.summary_en: In 2013, India introduced a "None of the Above" (NOTA) option on ballots — a pure protest vote. This paper uses the reform to answer two questions: who are protest voters, and do they matter? Structural estimation shows that without a NOTA option, most protest voters simply abstain. Those who do turn out spread their votes thinly across many candidates and have almost no impact on who wins. From a policy standpoint, NOTA is an effective tool for raising turnout (including among dissatisfied voters) and could ease some of the distortions created by compulsory voting in other countries.
papers.data_used: Structural model estimated on Indian parliamentary and state election data pre- and post-2013 NOTA reform. Voting records, candidate shares, turnout, and the NOTA share.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian debates on ballot design and voter engagement. Introducing a NOTA option would likely raise engagement among dissatisfied voters without distorting outcomes. Relevant for NVB (National Election Office) and for legislative debates on ballot design. Caveat: India's political system differs markedly from Hungary's (many candidates, first-past-the-post). Magnitudes and the turnout effect in a Hungarian mixed system would be different; a calibration to Hungarian polling data would be a useful next step.
policy_instruments: ballot design (None of the Above option)

papers.title: SKILL REQUIREMENTS, SEARCH FRICTIONS, AND WAGE INEQUALITY*
papers.abstract: We analyze wage inequality, extending the Burdett and Mortensen ( International Economic Review 39 (1998), 257–73) model by incorporating worker heterogeneity through skill requirements. We provide sufficient conditions for existence of an equilibrium where more productive firms offer higher wages. The unique such equilibrium is characterized in a closed form solution. Both within‐ and between‐group inequality are explicitly calculated. We then calibrate the model to explain the joint movement of both within‐ and between‐group inequality in the late 1980s and 1990s, an explanation that has been elusive in the literature so far.
papers.summary_en: Why did wage inequality rise so much within education groups and between education groups in the US during the 1980s-90s? This paper extends the classic Burdett-Mortensen search-model framework to include heterogeneous workers with different skill requirements. The authors derive a closed-form characterisation of the equilibrium and calibrate it to explain joint movements in within-group and between-group wage inequality — a puzzle previous literature had struggled with. A productive blend of labour-search theory and empirical calibration.
papers.data_used: Theoretical search model calibrated to US wage-distribution data, 1980s-90s. Uses standard CPS or equivalent wage micro-data.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian labour-market analysis, the model offers a framework to explain how minimum-wage compression and search frictions jointly shape the wage distribution. Relevant to MNB labour-market analysis and to KRTK wage-inequality research. Caveat: US-calibrated; Hungarian institutional features (strong collective-bargaining coverage, sector-specific wage floors) would shift the quantitative predictions.

papers.title: Measuring factor income shares at the sectoral level
Estimating the economic impacts of knowledge network and entrepreneurship development in smart specialization policy
papers.abstract: An undesirable result of the rapid implementation of smart specialization into the framework of European Union Cohesion Policy was that it left several practical issues unanswered. An important unanswered issue is the implementation of economic impact assessment in a smart specialization policy context. Integrating entrepreneurship and interregional network policies into an economic modelling framework is considered among the most prominent challenges. This paper introduces how these two policies are implemented in the GMR-Europe (geographic, macro and regional) model. The simulations highlight that smart specialization policy targeting the development of entrepreneurship and knowledge networks is not equally successful in all regions.
papers.summary_en: When the EU's Smart Specialisation Strategy was rolled out, it raised an unsolved practical problem: how to assess the economic impact of policies that target both knowledge networks and entrepreneurship development jointly? Varga, Sebestyén, Szerb and Szabó-Bartha integrate these two policy components into a regional economic-modelling framework — combining geographical economics with knowledge-spillover networks. They demonstrate how the framework can be used to estimate impacts of specific policy mixes on regional output and employment, applied to selected European regions. Provides a methodological building block for Cohesion Policy programme assessments that previously had to handle the components separately.
papers.summary_hu: Az EU Okos Szakosodási Stratégiájának bevezetése egy gyakorlati problémát hagyott megoldatlanul: hogyan mérjük olyan politikák gazdasági hatását, amelyek a tudáshálózatokat és a vállalkozásfejlesztést egyszerre célozzák? Varga, Sebestyén, Szerb és Szabó-Bartha integrálja ezt a két szakpolitikai komponenst egy regionális gazdasági modellezési keretbe — ötvözve a földrajzi gazdaságtant tudás-átgyűrűzési hálózatokkal. Bemutatják, hogyan használható a keret konkrét szakpolitikai mixek regionális kibocsátásra és foglalkoztatásra gyakorolt hatásának becslésére, kiválasztott európai régiókra alkalmazva. Módszertani építőkövet biztosít a Kohéziós Politikai program-értékelésekhez, amelyeknek korábban a komponenseket külön kellett kezelniük.
papers.data_used: Regional NUTS-2 / NUTS-3 panel data on output, employment, R&D spending, knowledge networks (patent-co-invention or research-collaboration measures). Geographical economic model calibrated to selected EU regions.
papers.data_used_hu: Regionális NUTS-2 / NUTS-3 paneladatok a kibocsátásra, foglalkoztatásra, K+F-kiadásokra, tudáshálózatokra (szabadalom-társfeltalálási vagy kutatási együttműködési mértékek). Kiválasztott EU-régiókra kalibrált földrajzi gazdasági modell.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian Smart Specialization Strategy (S3) implementation under ESIF: the paper offers a tool for ex-ante and ex-post assessment of S3 spending. Useful at the National Smart Specialization Authority level. Caveat: the model requires substantial regional knowledge-network data which is harder to assemble for Hungary's smaller NUTS-3 regions; specific Hungarian application would require additional data work.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: A magyar Okos Szakosodási Stratégia (S3) végrehajtásához az ESIF alatt: a tanulmány eszközt kínál az S3-kiadások ex-ante és ex-post értékeléséhez. Hasznos a Nemzeti Okos Szakosodási Hatóság szintjén. Korlát: a modell jelentős regionális tudáshálózat-adatokat igényel, amelyek Magyarország kisebb NUTS-3 régióira nehezebben összegyűjthetők; a konkrét magyar alkalmazás további adatmunkát igényel.

papers.title: Repeated common value auctions with asymmetric bidders
Efficiency and competition in the long run: The survival of the unfit
High profit equilibria in directed search models
Competing auctions: Finite markets and convergence
papers.abstract: The literature on competing auctions offers a model where sellers compete for buyers by setting reserve prices freely. An important outstanding conjecture (e.g. Peters and Severinov (1997)) is that the sellers post prices close to their marginal costs when the market becomes large. This conjecture is confirmed in this paper. More precisely, we show that if all sellers have zero costs, then the equilibrium reserve price converges to 0 in distribution. I also show that if there is a high enough lower bound on the buyers’ valuations, then there is a symmetric pure strategy equilibrium. In this equilibrium, if the number of buyers (sellers) increases, then the equilibrium reserve price increases (decreases) and the reserve price is decreasing in the size of the market.
papers.summary_en: When sellers compete for buyers by setting reserve prices in auctions, do the reserve prices approach marginal cost as the market grows large? This paper confirms a long-standing conjecture in the competing-auctions literature: yes. More precisely, if all sellers have zero costs, the equilibrium reserve price converges in distribution to zero as the market becomes large. If buyer valuations are bounded below sufficiently high, a symmetric pure-strategy equilibrium exists; in that equilibrium, more buyers push reserve prices up and more sellers push them down, with reserve prices decreasing in overall market size.
papers.summary_hu: Amikor az eladók versenyeznek a vevőkért az aukciókban, szabadon beállítva a kikiáltási árakat, közelednek-e a kikiáltási árak a marginális költséghez, ahogy a piac nagy lesz? A tanulmány megerősít egy régi sejtést a versengő-aukciós irodalomban: igen. Pontosabban, ha minden eladónak nulla költsége van, az egyensúlyi kikiáltási ár eloszlásban nullához konvergál, ahogy a piac naggyá válik. Ha a vevői értékelések alsó korlátja elég magas, létezik szimmetrikus tiszta-stratégiás egyensúly; abban az egyensúlyban több vevő felfelé nyomja a kikiáltási árakat és több eladó lefelé, a kikiáltási árak a piacméretben csökkenőek.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; nincs empirikus adat.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian online-marketplace and platform-competition policy (GVH): supports the case that large platforms with many competing sellers do converge on competitive outcomes, which is relevant to merger analysis and platform-regulation debates. Less supportive of intervention in platforms that already have many sellers per buyer than in those with few. Abstract; concrete assessment of a specific marketplace requires empirical calibration.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: A magyar online-piactér- és platform-verseny-politikához (GVH): támogatja azt az érvelést, hogy a sok versenyző eladóval rendelkező nagy platformok konvergálnak versenyképes kimenetelekhez, ami releváns a fúzió-elemzéshez és a platform-szabályozási vitákhoz. Kevésbé támogatja a beavatkozást olyan platformokon, amelyek már sok eladóval rendelkeznek vevőnként, mint azokon, amelyekkel kevés. Absztrakt; konkrét piac-felmérés empirikus kalibrációt igényel.

papers.title: First-price auctions with resale: the case of many bidders
Auctions with resale: Reserve prices and revenues
Post-war reconstruction and the Golden Age of economic growth
papers.abstract: This article briefly reviews the core literature on the Golden Age of economic growth and tests the explanatory power of alternative theories against one another, with particular emphasis on the reconstruction thesis as developed by Jánossy. While previous empirical work on the subject relied on cross-sectional analysis, I employ panel-data techniques, which produce more robust estimates. I demonstrate that, for the core western industrialised nations, the rapidity and variety of economic growth during the 1950s and 1960s can mostly be explained by post-war reconstruction, the completion of which marked the end of the Golden Age. Labour-force expansion also made a very strong positive contribution. In the more peripheral countries of the OECD, however, rapid catching-up from the late 1950s was largely brought about by structural modernisation. Finally, human-capital accumulation has had a determining impact on long-run growth potentials, modelled here as time-constant country-fixed effects.
papers.summary_en: Why did Western Europe grow so fast in the 1950s and 1960s — and why did the boom end? Vonyó tests competing explanations of the post-war 'Golden Age' against each other, with particular focus on Jánossy's reconstruction thesis. Using panel-data methods (an upgrade on prior cross-sectional work), he finds that for the core industrial nations, post-war reconstruction explains most of the rapid 1950s–60s growth, and the end of that reconstruction marks the end of the Golden Age. Labour-force expansion also contributed strongly. In peripheral OECD countries, structural modernisation drove the catch-up. Human capital matters for long-run potential but not for the timing of the boom.
papers.summary_hu: Miért nőtt olyan gyorsan Nyugat-Európa az 1950-es és 1960-as években — és miért ért véget a fellendülés? Vonyó panelen teszteli a 'Golden Age'-re adott versengő magyarázatokat, különös tekintettel Jánossy újjáépítési tézisére (a panelmódszer korszerűsítés a korábbi keresztmetszeti munkához képest). Az ipari magországokra nézve a háború utáni újjáépítés magyarázza a gyors 1950–60-as növekedést, és ennek lezárulása jelzi a Golden Age végét. A munkaerő-bővülés szintén erős hozzájárulást adott. A periferiális OECD-országokban a szerkezeti modernizáció hajtotta a felzárkózást. Az emberi tőke a hosszú távú potenciálnál fontos, de a fellendülés időzítését nem magyarázza.
papers.data_used: Panel of ~15 European OECD countries, 1950–1985 annual: GDP, capital stock, labour force, human capital. Builds on Maddison historical series with corrections.
papers.data_used_hu: ~15 európai OECD-ország paneladatai 1950–1985, éves frekvencia: GDP, tőkeállomány, munkaerő, emberi tőke. Maddison történeti idősorra épít korrekciókkal.
papers.policy_relevance: Useful framing for current debates on Hungary's mid-2030s growth trajectory: if Hungary is in a 'transition reconstruction' phase rather than a 'modernised steady state', the paper suggests reconstruction-style growth tails off endogenously, not because of policy mistakes. For NGM long-run-growth scenarios and the MNB Growth Report, distinguish the catch-up component from the steady-state component when projecting.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Hasznos keret a magyar 2030-as évek növekedési pályájáról szóló jelenlegi vitákhoz: ha Magyarország 'rendszerváltási újjáépítési' fázisban van, nem pedig 'modernizált állandósult állapotban', a tanulmány szerint az újjáépítési típusú növekedés endogén módon kifut, nem szakpolitikai hibák miatt. Az NGM hosszú távú növekedési forgatókönyveihez és az MNB Növekedési Jelentéséhez érdemes elválasztani a felzárkózási komponenst az állandósult-állapot komponenstől a vetítésnél.

papers.title: The bombing of Germany: the economic geography of war-induced dislocation in West German industry
papers.abstract: This paper reveals the impact of wartime destruction in urban housing on regional economic growth in West Germany between 1939 and 1950. I demonstrate econometrically that the German economy remained severely dislocated as long as the urban housing stock had not been rebuilt. The recovery of urban industry was constrained by a war-induced labour shortage and, therefore, industrial capacities remained underutilized. In contrast, the growth of the rural economy was facilitated by labour expansion, which depressed industrial labour productivity. I apply instrumental variables to account for endogeneity and robust regressions to adjust for the impact of outliers.
papers.summary_en: How does wartime destruction shape regional growth a decade later? Vonyó isolates the impact of urban housing destruction on West German economic geography, 1939–1950. Using city-level data and instrumental variables to address the endogeneity of bombing patterns, he shows that the West German economy remained severely dislocated as long as urban housing was unrebuilt: industrial recovery was held back by a war-induced labour shortage in cities (workers couldn't return without housing), while the rural economy expanded as displaced workers settled there, depressing industrial labour productivity. A natural-experiment view of the housing-labour-mobility-productivity nexus.
papers.summary_hu: Hogyan alakítja a háborús pusztítás a regionális növekedést egy évtizeddel később? Vonyó izolálja a városi lakásállomány-pusztulás hatását a nyugatnémet gazdaságföldrajzra 1939–1950 között. Városszintű adatokkal és instrumentális változókkal kezelve a bombázási minták endogenitását, kimutatja, hogy a nyugatnémet gazdaság mindaddig súlyosan diszlokált maradt, amíg a városi lakásállományt nem építették újjá: az ipari helyreállítást háború okozta városi munkaerőhiány fékezte (a munkások lakás nélkül nem térhettek vissza), míg a vidéki gazdaság bővült, ahogy a kitelepítettek ott telepedtek le, csökkentve az ipari munkatermelékenységet. Természetes-kísérlet pillantás a lakás-munkaerő-mobilitás-termelékenység csomópontra.
papers.data_used: City-level panel for West Germany, 1939–1950, on housing stock, industrial output, labour force, war damage. IVs based on bombing-target characteristics.
papers.data_used_hu: Nyugat-németországi városszintű panel 1939–1950 között: lakásállomány, ipari kibocsátás, munkaerő, háborús kár. IV-ek a bombázási célpontok jellemzőire.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevance for present-day post-conflict reconstruction policy (Ukraine) and for housing-policy debates in regions losing population: housing stock is a fundamental binding constraint on labour mobility and therefore on productive capacity. For Hungarian regional policy, the lesson is that cheap rural housing without accompanying urban housing investment can lock workers into low-productivity locations. Caveat: the 1940s policy environment is far from today's; quantitative magnitudes don't transfer directly.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Relevancia a jelenkori posztkonfliktusos újjáépítési politikára (Ukrajna) és a népességet vesztő régiók lakáspolitikai vitáira: a lakásállomány alapvető szűk keresztmetszet a munkaerő-mobilitásra, és ezáltal a termelőkapacitásra. A magyar regionális politikára vonatkozó tanulság: az olcsó vidéki lakás városi lakásberuházás nélkül alacsony termelékenységű helyekre láncolhatja a munkásokat. Korlát: az 1940-es évek policy-környezete távol áll a maitól; a kvantitatív nagyságok nem közvetlenül átvihetők.

papers.title: DavidGreasley and LesOxley, eds., Economics and history: surveys in cliometrics (Oxford, Wiley‐Blackwell, 2012. Pp. 304. ISBN 9781444337808 Pbk. £19.99)
War and socialism: why eastern Europe fell behind between 1950 and 1989†
papers.abstract: This article reconsiders the relative growth performance of centrally planned economies in the broader context of postwar growth in Europe. It reports a new dataset of revised estimates for investment rates in eastern European countries between 1950 and 1989. Complemented with data on other growth determinants, this evidence is used to re‐evaluate the socialist growth record in a conditional convergence framework with a panel of 24 European countries. After controlling for relative backwardness, investment rates, and improvements in human capital, the findings show that centrally planned economies underperformed due to their relative inefficiency only after the postwar golden age. In the 1950s and 1960s, eastern Europe was falling behind mainly due to relatively low levels of investment and weak reconstruction dynamics. Both are explained, in part, by the lack of labour‐supply flexibility that, in turn, resulted from the comparatively much larger negative impact of the war on population growth in eastern Europe.
papers.summary_en: Why did Eastern Europe fall behind Western Europe under socialism? The standard answer is 'inefficiency' — but for which decades? Vonyó constructs a new dataset of revised investment-rate estimates for centrally planned economies, 1950–1989, and re-evaluates the socialist growth record in a conditional-convergence framework across 24 European countries. The result splits the post-war era. After the Golden Age (1970s–1980s), centrally planned economies underperformed because of inefficiency. But in the 1950s and 1960s, the Eastern shortfall was driven mainly by lower investment rates and weak reconstruction dynamics — and the latter was partly because the war's population shock was much larger in the East. Inefficiency is a story for the late socialist era, not the whole.
papers.summary_hu: Miért szakadt le Kelet-Európa Nyugattól a szocializmus alatt? A standard válasz: 'hatékonyság-hiány' — de mely évtizedekre? Vonyó új adatállományt épít a tervgazdaságok 1950–1989 közötti felülvizsgált beruházási rátáira, és 24 európai országból álló panelen újraértékeli a szocialista növekedési teljesítményt feltételes konvergencia-keretben. Az eredmény kettéosztja a háború utáni korszakot. A Golden Age után (1970-es és 80-as évek) a tervgazdaságok hatékonyság-hiány miatt teljesítettek alul. De az 1950-es és 60-as években a keleti lemaradás főleg az alacsonyabb beruházási rátákból és a gyenge újjáépítési dinamikából fakadt — utóbbi részben azért, mert a háború népességi sokkja sokkal nagyobb volt keleten. A hatékonyság-hiány a kései szocialista korszak története, nem az egész szocializmusé.
papers.data_used: Panel of 24 European economies, 1950–1989, with revised investment rates from new archival work for centrally planned economies (CZ, HU, PL, GDR, USSR), GDP per capita, human capital indicators, war-induced population losses.
papers.data_used_hu: 24 európai ország paneladatai 1950–1989, a tervgazdaságokra (CS, HU, PL, NDK, SZU) új levéltári munkából felülvizsgált beruházási rátákkal, egy főre jutó GDP, emberi-tőke mutatók, háború okozta népességveszteség.
papers.policy_relevance: Reframes the Hungarian transition narrative. The standard 'communism made us inefficient' framing is partly correct (for the 1980s) but mistakes 1950s slow growth for an institutional failure when it was largely a demographic one. For ongoing 'why is Hungary still behind Austria' debates, the paper suggests the relevant comparison era is the 1970s–80s, not the immediate postwar period. Useful framing for KSH historical national-accounts work and for MNB long-run growth analysis.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Újrakeretezi a magyar rendszerváltási narratívát. A standard 'a kommunizmus tett bennünket nem-hatékonnyá' keret részben helytálló (az 1980-as évekre), de tévesen intézményi kudarcnak véli az 1950-es évek lassú növekedését, amikor az nagyrészt demográfiai volt. A 'miért van Magyarország még mindig Ausztria mögött' aktuális vitákban a tanulmány szerint a releváns összehasonlítási korszak az 1970–80-as évek, nem a közvetlen háború utáni időszak. Hasznos keret a KSH történeti nemzeti-számla munkájához és az MNB hosszú távú növekedési elemzéséhez.

papers.title: Made in Germany: The Post-War Export Boom
papers.abstract: A summary is not available for this content so a preview has been provided. Please use the Get access link above for information on how to access this content.

papers.title: Technology and the Environment in State-Socialist Hungary: An Economic History. By Viktor Pál. London: Palgrave Macmillan, 2017. Pp. xiv, 263. $151.53, cloth; $116.92, eBook.
papers.abstract: An abstract is not available for this content so a preview has been provided. Please use the Get access link above for information on how to access this content.

papers.title: Why did socialist economies fail? The role of factor inputs reconsidered
papers.abstract: Abstract This article presents new estimates for investment and new growth accounts for three socialist economies between 1950 and 1989. Government statistics reported distorted measures for both the rate and the trajectory of productivity growth in Czechoslovakia, Hungary, and Poland. Researchers have benefited from revised output data, but have continued to use official statistics on capital input, or estimated capital stock from official investment data. Investment levels and rates of capital accumulation were much lower than officially claimed and over‐reporting worsened over time. A setback in factor accumulation—both investment in equipment and labour input—contributed very significantly to the socialist growth failure of the 1980s.
papers.summary_en: If Eastern European socialist economies reported distorted output statistics, how distorted were their inputs? Vonyó and Klein construct new investment estimates and growth accounts for Czechoslovakia, Hungary and Poland, 1950–1989. Their finding: official capital-input statistics overstated investment levels and rates of accumulation, and the over-reporting worsened over time. Once the inputs are corrected, the standard productivity-residual analysis changes substantially: a setback in factor accumulation — both equipment investment and labour input — contributed significantly to the 1980s socialist growth failure, not just declining technical efficiency.
papers.summary_hu: Ha a kelet-európai szocialista gazdaságok torzított kibocsátási statisztikákat közöltek, mennyire torzították a bemeneteket? Vonyó és Klein új beruházási becsléseket és növekedési számvitelt épít Csehszlovákiára, Magyarországra és Lengyelországra 1950–1989 között. Eredményük: a hivatalos tőke-bemenet statisztikák felülbecsülték a beruházási szinteket és a felhalmozás ütemét, és az ferdítés idővel romlott. Ha a bemeneteket korrigáljuk, a standard termelékenységi-reziduum elemzés lényegesen megváltozik: a tényezőfelhalmozás visszaesése — mind a gépberuházás, mind a munka bemenet — jelentősen hozzájárult az 1980-as évek szocialista növekedési kudarcához, nem csak a hanyatló műszaki hatékonyság.
papers.data_used: New revised investment series and capital-stock estimates for Czechoslovakia, Hungary, Poland, 1950–1989, derived from archival sources. Output and labour-input data from existing revised series. Standard growth-accounting decomposition.
papers.data_used_hu: Új felülvizsgált beruházási idősorok és tőkeállomány-becslések Csehszlovákiára, Magyarországra és Lengyelországra 1950–1989-re, levéltári forrásokból. Kibocsátási és munkaerő-bemeneti adatok meglévő felülvizsgált sorokból. Standard növekedési-számviteli felbontás.
papers.policy_relevance: Important methodological note for KSH historical national accounts — the 1950s–80s investment series in the official record need correction for serious upward bias. Affects every empirical paper that uses uncorrected pre-1990 capital input. For long-run growth research at MNB / KRTK, the paper signals that 'pre-transition' productivity gaps are smaller than commonly assumed and that the Hungarian transition's productivity gain is correspondingly smaller too.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Fontos módszertani figyelmeztetés a KSH történelmi nemzeti számláihoz — a hivatalos rekord 1950–80-as évekbeli beruházási idősorai komoly felfelé torzítást igényelnek korrigálni. Minden olyan empirikus tanulmányt érint, amely korrigálatlan 1990 előtti tőke-bemenetet használ. A hosszú távú növekedéskutatás számára (MNB, KRTK) a tanulmány azt jelzi, hogy a 'rendszerváltás előtti' termelékenységi rések kisebbek a feltételezettnél, és a magyar rendszerváltás termelékenységi nyeresége ennek megfelelően kisebb.

papers.title: Economic growth and structural developments, 1945–1989
papers.abstract: This chapter focuses on the growth performance of the Central, East and South-East Europe (CESEE) countries based on the best available quantitative evidence on national income and its composition by sector. It presents an overview of aggregate growth trends based on gross domestic product per capital levels. The chapter focuses on the two factors that, according to the broader literature on post-war growth, were fundamental to the productivity surge of the golden age: structural modernization and capital accumulation. The 1950s and 1960s were the golden age of economic growth not only for Western industrialised nations but also for centrally planned economies in Eastern Europe. Capital accumulation is central to modern economic growth and played an instrumental role in the growth of European economies after 1945. CESEE countries attained unprecedented rates of economic growth during the post-war era, demonstrating common general patterns of development.
papers.summary_en: A reference chapter on the growth performance of Central, Eastern and South-Eastern European (CESEE) countries from 1945 to 1989. Vonyó and Markevich present the best-available quantitative evidence on national income and its sectoral composition. The analytical lens is the two factors most central to post-war growth literature: structural modernisation (movement of labour and capital from agriculture to industry) and capital accumulation. CESEE countries achieved unprecedented rates of growth during the post-war Golden Age (1950s–60s), with broad similarities in pattern to Western European catch-up. The chapter is the standard introductory synthesis for this region and period.
papers.summary_hu: Referenciafejezet a Közép-, Kelet- és Délkelet-Európa-i (CESEE) országok 1945 és 1989 közötti növekedési teljesítményéről. Vonyó és Markevich a nemzeti jövedelemre és annak ágazati összetételére vonatkozó legjobb elérhető kvantitatív bizonyítékokat mutatja be. Az elemzési optika a háború utáni növekedési irodalom két központi tényezője: szerkezeti modernizáció (a munkaerő és tőke átáramlása mezőgazdaságról iparra) és tőkefelhalmozás. A CESEE-országok példa nélküli növekedési ütemet értek el a háború utáni Golden Age (1950–60-as évek) idején, a nyugat-európai felzárkózáshoz hasonló mintákkal. A fejezet a régió és időszak standard bevezető szintézise.
papers.data_used: Synthesised national-accounts data for ~12 CESEE countries, 1945–1989, drawn from Maddison Project, Eastern Europe historical statistical archives, and revised series in Vonyó's earlier work. GDP, sectoral output, capital stock, labour force.
papers.data_used_hu: Szintetizált nemzeti számla adatok ~12 CESEE-országra 1945–1989, a Maddison Projektből, kelet-európai történeti statisztikai levéltárakból és Vonyó korábbi munkáinak felülvizsgált sorozataiból. GDP, ágazati kibocsátás, tőkeállomány, munkaerő.
papers.policy_relevance: Foundational reference for any Hungarian long-run-growth analysis stretching across the 1945-1989 period. Useful for KRTK economic-history work and for MNB historical decompositions. Caveat: a chapter-length synthesis; specific quantitative claims should be cross-checked against the underlying papers (notably the Vonyó & Klein 2018 piece on the same period).
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Alapozó hivatkozás minden olyan magyar hosszú távú növekedéselemzéshez, amely 1945–1989-re terjed ki. Hasznos a KRTK gazdaságtörténeti munkáihoz és az MNB történelmi felbontásaihoz. Korlát: fejezet-hosszúságú szintézis; a konkrét kvantitatív állításokat ellenőrizni kell az alapul szolgáló tanulmányokkal szemben (különösen a Vonyó és Klein 2018-as munkájával ugyanerre az időszakra).

papers.title: Entangled Financial Systems
papers.abstract: I model an entangled financial system in which banks hedge their portfolio risks using over-the-counter (OTC) contracts. However, banks choose not to hedge counterparty risk, and thus the idiosyncratic failure of a bank can lead to a systemic run of lenders. An inefficiency arises because banks engage in a version of risk shifting through the network externalities created by OTC contracts. Banks do not take into account that the costly hedging of low-probability counterparty risk also benefits other banks. In the model, it is welfare improving to tax OTC contracts to finance a bailout fund. The Author 2013. Published by Oxford University Press on behalf of The Society for Financial Studies. All rights reserved. For Permissions, please e-mail: journals.permissions@oup.com., Oxford University Press.
papers.summary_en: When banks use over-the-counter (OTC) derivatives to hedge portfolio risks, they create a dense web of counterparty exposures. This paper shows that even if each bank hedges its own portfolio, banks choose not to hedge counterparty risk — leading to systemic vulnerability. The idiosyncratic failure of one bank can trigger a lender run that sweeps through the network. An inefficiency arises because banks don't internalise the externality that costly counterparty hedging benefits others. The paper shows that taxing OTC contracts to finance a bailout fund is welfare-improving. A compact theoretical foundation for post-2008 macroprudential regulation.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data. Directly motivated by 2008 financial-crisis OTC counterparty-risk patterns.
papers.policy_relevance: Foundational for Hungarian macroprudential policy (MNB). The paper's welfare case for OTC derivative taxation and explicit bailout-fund pre-financing maps directly onto EU regulation (EMIR, BRRD, resolution funds). Supports MNB's stance on concentrated counterparty risk and on pre-funded resolution mechanisms. Caveat: the model is stylised; Hungarian banking concentration and EU resolution-fund design set the quantitative context.

papers.title: Kezelési költségük határozza-e meg a Magyarországon forgalmazott részvénypiaci befektetési alapok teljesítményét?
papers.abstract: E tanulmány azt vizsgálja, hogy a kezelési költségekkel mennyiben magyarázható a Magyarországon forgalmazott részvénypiaci befektetési alapok teljesítménye. Az alapspecifikáció szerint, ha egy alapnak 1 százalékponttal magasabb a kezelési költsége, akkor az alap átlagosan több mint 1 százalékponttal rosszabbul teljesít a referenciahozamhoz képest (Jensen alfája). A szerző megmutatja, hogy több alap, bár hivatalosan aktív befektetési stratégiát hirdet, valójában csak az adott indexet követi. Továbbá arra is rávilágít, hogy milyen nehézségeket okoz a teljesítmény mérésében, hogy a Budapesti Értéktőzsde által létrehozott CETOP referenciaindex nem tartalmazza az osztalékokat, és kitér ennek orvoslására is.* Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: G23, G12.

papers.title: A comparison of European, Polish, Slovenian and British EQ-5D-3L value sets using a Hungarian sample of 18 chronic diseases
papers.abstract: Our results suggest that the choice of value set largely influences the health state utility results in chronic diseases, and might have a significant impact on health policy decisions.
papers.summary_en: The EQ-5D-3L instrument measures health-state utility for cost-effectiveness analysis. But the "value set" (which assigns utility to each state) can differ across countries. Does the choice matter for concrete policy decisions? This paper compares European, Polish, Slovenian, and British value sets on a Hungarian sample of patients with 18 chronic diseases. The conclusion: value-set choice substantially influences health-state utility results in chronic disease — and thus affects health-policy decisions. An important methodological caution for Hungarian cost-effectiveness analyses.
papers.data_used: Hungarian sample of patients with 18 chronic diseases, evaluated against European, Polish, Slovenian, and British EQ-5D-3L value sets.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian health-technology assessment (GYEMSZI, NEAK) and to drug-reimbursement decisions. The paper supports the development of a Hungarian-specific value set for EQ-5D-3L to avoid systematic biases from using foreign sets. Relevant to Hungarian HTA methodology reform. Caveat: chronic-disease focus; acute and preventive-care implications may differ.

papers.title: Determinants of the acceptability of health problems in different ages: exploring a new application of the EQ VAS
papers.abstract: When measured with the EQ VAS, health problems were increasingly acceptable with age. Capturing well the individual variability in the assessment of acceptable health states at different ages, the EQ VAS is a useful addition to EQ-5D-3L descriptive system-based measures of acceptable health.
papers.summary_en: Ageing changes what health problems people find acceptable — older people often accept worse health than younger ones. This paper explores the EQ-VAS (visual-analogue-scale) for measuring the acceptability of health problems across ages. Results: health problems are increasingly acceptable with age, and the EQ VAS captures individual variability well. A methodological contribution to health-state measurement useful for health-policy resource allocation when age-acceptability patterns matter.
papers.data_used: Survey data on health-state acceptability across ages using the EQ VAS.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian health-technology assessment (GYEMSZI, NEAK) and for age-differentiated healthcare-resource decisions. Relevant to discussions about age-adjusted quality-adjusted life years (QALYs) in cost-effectiveness analysis. Caveat: methodology paper; applied implications depend on whether Hungarian HTA adopts age-weighted QALYs.

policy.title: Policy brief on employment rehabilitation
Policy brief on long-term unemployment
Policy brief on early tracking in education
policy.summary_en: Policy brief on early educational tracking in Hungary. Reviews the international evidence that early sorting widens between-school inequality without raising mean achievement, and applies it to the Hungarian eight-year and six-year gimnázium system. Shows that early-tracking effects compound through the secondary cycle and contribute to the very high inter-school variance in Hungarian PISA scores.
policy.policy_relevance: Direct input for the Hungarian Education Ministry / OH on secondary-school structure. Argues for delaying tracking and for stronger general-education content in the early secondary years. Politically charged in Hungary because it conflicts with the elite-gimnázium system. Caveat: the international evidence has heterogeneity that the Hungarian application doesn't fully reconcile.

policy.title: Policy brief on using administrative data to improve policy making in the CEE
Impact of increased local cooperation on PES outreach to non-employed youths in Hungary
Policy lessons from the evaluation of youth employment policies in Spain, Hungary, Italy and Poland
Policy brief on access to and quality of public data on EU funds
Disability insurance reform in Hungary: Fiscal gains at human cost
policy.summary_en: Case study of Hungary's 2011–2012 disability-insurance reform, which sharply tightened eligibility and re-assessed the incumbent disability-pension caseload. The fiscal saving was large, but documented human costs — re-assessed beneficiaries with worse health and lower earnings post-reform — were significant. The paper compiles the evidence and quantifies the trade-off.
policy.policy_relevance: Direct relevance to ongoing Hungarian disability and rehabilitation policy (KSH, Pénzügyminisztérium, NÉP). The central message: tightening eligibility delivers fiscal gains but transfers costs to a vulnerable group; pairing with rehabilitation services materially reduces the human cost. Useful for parliamentary testimony and EU-level disability policy reviews. Caveat: data limitations on long-run health outcomes.

policy.title: Ending homelessness in CEE. Comparative report
Policy brief on ending homelessness — Hungary
How can Public Employment Services act to reach NEETs? Comparative report on four countries
Measuring competitiveness in Europe: resource allocation, granularity and trade
policy.summary_en: Co-edited Bruegel Blueprint XXIV synthesising the CompNet research network's findings on European competitiveness. Brings together micro-aggregated firm-level evidence on productivity dispersion, resource misallocation, granularity (big-firm dominance), and trade. Hungary appears throughout as one of the most-analysed CEE benchmarks.
policy.policy_relevance: Frame document for the European Commission and ECB on competitiveness measurement: a single TFP number per country is not enough; misallocation and granularity matter. Hungarian policymakers (MNB, MGFM, NKFI) can use the framework to interpret why headline productivity gaps with the EU15 are not closing. Caveat: large multi-author synthesis; specific channels for Hungary need detail beyond the headline measures.

policy.title: Measuring competitiveness in a granular and global world (chapter)
policy.outlet: Bruegel Blueprint #24 — Measuring competitiveness in Europe
policy.summary_en: Opening chapter of Blueprint XXIV. Argues for a granular and global view of competitiveness: aggregate measures average over the relevant heterogeneity, and trade matters because it concentrates competitiveness pressure on specific firms and products. Lays the groundwork for the firm-level chapters that follow.
policy.policy_relevance: Methodological frame for the European Commission's competitiveness scorecards and the MNB's productivity work: headline TFP and ULC numbers should be supplemented with within-country dispersion measures and trade-exposed-segment indicators. Caveat: a methodological piece, not a policy-recommendations chapter.

policy.title: Motives of corporate location choice (chapter)
policy.outlet: The Hungarian Labour Market 2004 (Fazekas, Koltay, Cseres-Gergely eds.)

policy.title: Barriers to data access and matching in Europe
policy.summary_en: Chapter in the Castellani-Koch volume *Mapping competitiveness with European data*. Surveys the European firm-level data landscape and documents the practical and legal barriers to linking national firm registers across countries. The Hungarian case is featured as one of the better-organised national datasets, but cross-border matching remains painful.
policy.policy_relevance: Useful for KSH and Eurostat: the lack of linked firm data is a binding constraint on EU-wide competitiveness analysis. Supports the case for legal and technical investment in cross-border microdata access (CompNet, EuropeanDataLab). Caveat: written before GDPR, so the legal landscape has shifted.

policy.title: Still Standing: how European firms weathered the crisis
policy.summary_en: European firms weathered the 2008–9 crisis better than the macro data alone would suggest. Using the EFIGE survey of about 14,000 firms across seven EU countries (incl. Hungary), the report documents that exporters and multi-product firms were systematically more resilient. Hungarian results echo the European pattern: firms with stronger international links pre-crisis lost fewer workers and recovered faster.
policy.policy_relevance: Direct input for Hungarian export-promotion (HIPA) and SME-finance policy. Argues that firm-level internationalisation is itself a stabiliser, so the policy levers worth using during a downturn are export-credit access, trade finance, and FDI promotion — not blanket bailouts. Caveat: a single cross-section, so the resilience pattern may not generalise to demand-driven crises (e.g. COVID-19).

policy.title: How did exporting firms cope with the crisis? — EFIGE Country Report: Hungary
policy.summary_en: Hungary-specific country report from the EFIGE project. Documents how Hungarian exporting firms coped with the 2008–9 crisis: exporters did not exit at higher rates than non-exporters, but their export volumes contracted sharply, especially for single-product firms. Trade-credit constraints and demand shocks dominate the explanation; productivity differences alone cannot account for the patterns.
policy.policy_relevance: Crisis-time policy lessons for the Hungarian government and EU institutions: trade finance and export-credit insurance matter more than corporate income tax or labour-market policy in stabilising the export base. Supports the case for the Eximbank and EU-level crisis trade-credit facilities. Caveat: the report is descriptive; causal identification of the channels would need follow-up work.

policy.title: Micro-founded measurement of regional competitiveness in Europe (chapter)
policy.outlet: Bruegel Blueprint #24 — Measuring competitiveness in Europe
policy.summary_en: Chapter in Blueprint XXIV. Builds a micro-founded measure of regional competitiveness in Europe by aggregating firm-level productivity data within NUTS-2 regions. Shows that headline regional rankings move substantially when using micro-foundations vs. simple GVA-per-worker, and identifies which Hungarian and CEE regions are systematically over- or under-rated by the standard approach.
policy.policy_relevance: Direct input for EU Cohesion Policy targeting and for the Hungarian RPM (regional development) strategy. The micro-founded ranking would change which regions appear 'competitive' on the EU dashboard, with consequences for how Cohesion funds are allocated. Caveat: depends on the availability of harmonised firm data — Hungarian regions are well-covered, but smaller member states are not.

policy.title: Successful industrial policy must account for political economy
policy.summary_en: Plain-language synthesis of the new industrial-policy research for IMF Finance & Development. Argues that industrial policy is back on the global agenda — IRA, EU Green Deal, China's Made-in-China 2025 — and that successful design depends less on the choice of instruments and more on the political economy of the implementing state: capacity, accountability, and resistance to capture.
policy.policy_relevance: Reading-list item for any Hungarian ministry contemplating sectoral subsidies (battery, automotive, semiconductor). The framework's central warning — that capture risk dominates instrument choice — is directly relevant to the political economy of Hungarian industrial policy. Caveat: high-level synthesis, not a country case.

policy.title: Industriepolitik
Roma Employment at the Turn of the Millennium. An Analysis of the Nationwide Representative Roma Survey of 2003
policy.outlet: The Hungarian Labour Market 2010 (Fazekas, Lovász, Telegdy eds.)
policy.summary_en: Chapter analysing Roma employment patterns at the turn of the millennium, drawing on the 2003 nationally representative Roma survey. Documents very large employment-rate gaps — Roma employment well below half the non-Roma rate, with the gap concentrated in low-skill, low-region settings. Most of the gap is explained by location, education, and the local-labour-market structure rather than direct labour-market discrimination alone.
policy.policy_relevance: Foundational evidence for any Hungarian Roma-employment policy (e.g. Hídépítő, Útravaló, EU Roma Framework). Argues that education investment and local-labour-market activation are necessary conditions; anti-discrimination measures alone are insufficient. Still cited as the canonical Hungarian Roma labour-market reference. Caveat: the survey is now ~20 years old; a refresh on the 2018+ TÁRKI Roma sample would be valuable.

policy.title: Segregation of Primary Schools in Hungary. A Descriptive Study Using Data from the National Assessment of Basic Competences of 2006
policy.summary_en: Descriptive analysis of school segregation in Hungarian primary schools using the National Assessment of Basic Competences (OKM) 2006 micro-data. Documents very high between-school Roma segregation that increased through the 2000s, with the segregation increase driven not by demographic shifts but by school-choice patterns — Roma students are increasingly concentrated in a small set of schools as non-Roma families exit.
policy.policy_relevance: Direct input for the Hungarian Education Ministry on the school-segregation question. Demonstrates that without active de-segregation policy (school-catchment rules, integrated-class incentives), Roma school segregation increases mechanically through parental choice. Caveat: 2006 data; the church-school expansion since 2010 has further shifted patterns and the analysis would need updating.

policy.title: Population ageing and strengthened progressivity of pensions: the case of Hungary
policy.title_hu: Népességöregedés és a nyugdíjak erősített progresszivitása: a magyar eset
policy.summary_en: In Hungary, sustaining the public pension system under rapid population ageing requires more than the conventional levers. Simonovits models seven reform scenarios in a multi-cohort framework calibrated to Hungarian wage and pension data and projects to 2070. Without reform, the balanced contribution rate must rise from 20% in 2015 to 28% by 2063; under an unchanged primary deficit, public debt could exceed 200% of GDP. Indexing the statutory retirement age to life expectancy holds the contribution rate at 22% rather than 28%, but is not sufficient on its own. The paper's central argument is that strengthening progressivity of initial pensions — narrowing the benefit-to-earnings ratio for high earners — is essential, both for fiscal balance and to offset the longevity gap (high earners live longer, so a more progressive system has a lower balanced contribution rate by the Chebyshev sum inequality).
policy.summary_hu: A magyar nyugdíjrendszer fenntarthatóságához a gyors népességöregedés mellett több kell, mint a hagyományos eszközök. Simonovits hét reformforgatókönyvet modellez egy többkohortos keretben, magyar bér- és nyugdíjadatokra kalibrálva, 2070-ig kivetítve. Reform nélkül a kiegyensúlyozó járulékkulcs a 2015-ös 20 százalékról 2063-ra 28 százalékra nő; változatlan elsődleges hiány mellett az államadósság a GDP 200 százalékát is meghaladhatja. A nyugdíjkorhatár élettartamhoz indexálása 22 százalékon stabilizálja a járulékkulcsot, de önmagában nem elég. A tanulmány központi állítása, hogy a kezdő nyugdíjak progresszivitásának erősítése — a magas keresetekhez tartozó nyugdíj/kereset arány csökkentése — elengedhetetlen, részben a fiskális egyensúly miatt, részben azért, hogy ellensúlyozza az élettartamrést (a magas keresetűek tovább élnek, így egy progresszívabb rendszerben alacsonyabb a kiegyensúlyozó járulékkulcs, a Csebisev-egyenlőtlenség értelmében).
policy.policy_relevance: For Hungarian policymakers (Ministry of National Economy, MNB, social insurance authorities), the paper is a direct critique of the OECD's 2024 Hungarian-pension reform proposal — which combined retirement-age indexation with proportional benefit cuts. Simonovits argues uniform proportional cuts disproportionately harm low-pension recipients and ignore the longevity gap: high earners live longer, so unprogressive systems extract more from the short-lived poor. His central recommendation: combine three levers — gradual contribution-rate increases, retirement-age indexation to life expectancy, AND a flatter ratio of high-to-low initial benefits. Pricing-versus-wage indexation of pensions in payment is a second-order question; the central choice is whether the pension formula itself becomes more progressive.
policy.policy_relevance_hu: A magyar döntéshozók (Nemzetgazdasági Minisztérium, MNB, ONYF) számára a tanulmány közvetlen kritikája az OECD 2024-es magyar nyugdíjreform-javaslatának — amely a korhatár indexálását arányos nyugdíjcsökkentéssel kombinálta. Simonovits érvelése szerint az egyenletes arányos csökkentés aránytalanul érinti az alacsony nyugdíjasokat, és figyelmen kívül hagyja az élettartamrést: a magas keresetűek tovább élnek, így a nem-progresszív rendszer többet von el a rövidebb élettartamú alacsony keresetűektől. Központi javaslata három eszköz együttes alkalmazása — fokozatos járulékemelés, a korhatár élettartamhoz indexálása ÉS a magas-alacsony kezdő nyugdíj arány lapításának erősítése. A folyósított nyugdíjak ár- vagy bérindexálása másodrendű kérdés; a központi választás az, hogy maga a nyugdíjképlet legyen-e progresszívabb.
